author: khajavi
body:
This pull request updates the GitHub Actions CI workflow to add automated documentation build and release steps. The main improvements are the introduction of jobs to build and publish documentation, and updates to job dependencies to ensure documentation is built and released alongside the main project.

**CI/CD workflow enhancements:**

* Added a new `buildDocs` job to `.github/workflows/ci.yml` that builds project documentation using SBT and checks the website build process. This job runs in parallel with other checks and tests.
* Updated the main `ci` job to depend on the completion of the new `buildDocs` job, ensuring documentation is built before the CI aggregate completes.

**Documentation publishing automation:**

* Introduced a `release-docs` job that runs after the main release. This job publishes the documentation to the NPM registry when a release is published or the workflow is manually dispatched. It includes steps for environment setup and uses SBT to publish docs.
* Renamed the `publish` job to `release` for clarity and updated dependencies accordingly.
title: Verify Compilation of Docs on PRs and Publish Docs on Releases

author: 987Nabil
body:
## Summary

Adds a new `mediatype` micro library providing MIME type support with:

- `MediaType` case class with full MIME type representation
- ~2,600 predefined types generated from mime-db with camelCase names
- `mediaType"..."` string interpolator with compile-time validation
- `MediaType.parse()` for runtime parsing
- `MediaType.forFileExtension()` for file extension lookup

## Features

### MediaType Case Class
```scala
final case class MediaType(
  mainType: String,
  subType: String,
  compressible: Boolean = false,
  binary: Boolean = false,
  fileExtensions: List[String] = Nil,
  extensions: Map[String, String] = Map.empty,
  parameters: Map[String, String] = Map.empty
)
```

### String Interpolator
```scala
import zio.blocks.mediatype._

// Maps to predefined instance
val json = mediaType"application/json"  // eq MediaTypes.application.json

// With parameters
val html = mediaType"text/html; charset=utf-8"

// Compile-time validation with clear error messages
mediaType""           // Error: Invalid media type: cannot be empty
mediaType"invalid"    // Error: Invalid media type: must contain '/' separator
```

### Runtime Parsing
```scala
MediaType.parse("application/json")  // Right(MediaTypes.application.json)
MediaType.parse("custom/type")       // Right(MediaType("custom", "type"))
```

### File Extension Lookup
```scala
MediaType.forFileExtension("json")  // Some(MediaTypes.application.json)
MediaType.forFileExtension("html")  // Some(MediaTypes.text.html)
```

## Technical Details

- Cross-platform: JVM + JS
- Cross-version: Scala 2.13 + 3.x
- Zero dependencies
- Uses shared package object with version-specific traits for macros
- 100% test coverage on non-generated code

## Related Issue

Closes #992
/claim #992
title: feat(mediatype): add media type micro library with compile-time validated interpolator

author: ghostdogpr
body:
Part of https://github.com/zio/zio-blocks/issues/979

The new version is slightly less performant but it doesn't matter much as this will normally be saved in a val.

Before :
```
Owner(List(Owner.Package("scala"), Owner.Package("collection"), Owner.Package("immutable")))
Owner(List(Owner.Package("com"), Owner.Package("example"), Owner.Term("Outer"), Owner.Term("Inner")))
```
After:
```
Owner.fromPackagePath("scala.collection.immutable")
Owner.fromPackagePath("com.example").term("Outer").term("Inner")
```
title: Reduce bytecode generated by Owner creation macro

author: ghostdogpr
body:
Closes https://github.com/zio/zio-blocks/issues/979

Exclude Product, Equals, Serializable and Mirror.* from the base types list in `TypeDefKind`. These standard library types are automatically synthesized and generate substantial bytecode in macro expansions, contributing to "Method Too Large" errors for complex ADTs.
title: Filter common base types from TypeDefKind to reduce generated bytecode

author: Godzilla675
body:
## Summary

Pure, serializable schema migration system for ZIO Schema 2 - enables evolving schemas over time with type-safe, reversible transformations that operate on `DynamicValue`.

### What's included

| Component | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `DynamicMigration` | Untyped interpreter: applies a list of `MigrationAction`s to a `DynamicValue` |
| `Migration[A, B]` | Typed wrapper with `apply` / `applyDynamic` / `reverse` / `++` |
| `MigrationAction` | 15-variant ADT (AddField, DropField, RenameField, TransformValue, Join, Split, ...) |
| `DynamicSchemaExpr` | Serializable expression language (Literal, Path, Arithmetic, Coerce, StringOps, ...) |
| `MigrationBuilder` | Fluent builder with `build` (validates) / `buildPartial` (skips validation) |
| `TrackedMigrationBuilder` | **Scala 3**: Compile-time field tracking via type-level Tuple accumulation |
| `MigrationComplete` | **Scala 3**: Evidence type class + macro for compile-time completeness validation |
| `MigrationValidator` | Simulates actions against source/target schemas to catch structural mismatches at build time |
| `MigrationSchemas` | `Schema[DynamicMigration]`, `Schema[MigrationAction]`, `Schema[DynamicSchemaExpr]` for full round-trip serialization |
| `Selector` + macros | Compile-time selector lambdas (`_.address.street`) to `DynamicOptic` paths (Scala 2 & 3) |
| `Schema.structural` | Scala 3 macro: derives `Schema` for refinement types (`{ def name: String }`) and union types |

## Examples

**Add a field with a default**
```scala
import zio.blocks.schema._
import zio.blocks.schema.migration._
import zio.blocks.schema.migration.MigrationBuilderSyntax._

@schema case class PersonV1(name: String, age: Int)
@schema case class PersonV2(name: String, age: Int, country: String)

val migration: Migration[PersonV1, PersonV2] =
  Migration.newBuilder[PersonV1, PersonV2]
    .addField(_.country, "US")
    .build
```

**Compile-time field tracking (Scala 3)**
```scala
// Compile error if you forget to handle a field!
val migration = MigrationBuilderSyntax.checkedBuilder[PersonV1, PersonV2]
  .addField(_.country, "US")
  .build  // compile-time + runtime validation

// Or via extension method:
val migration = Migration.newBuilder[PersonV1, PersonV2]
  .tracked
  .addField(_.country, "US")
  .build
```

**Rename + compose**
```scala
@schema case class PersonV3(fullName: String, age: Int, country: String)

val v2ToV3: Migration[PersonV2, PersonV3] =
  Migration.newBuilder[PersonV2, PersonV3]
    .renameField(_.name, _.fullName)
    .buildPartial

val v1ToV3: Migration[PersonV1, PersonV3] = migration ++ v2ToV3
```

**Structural schemas (Scala 3 only)**
```scala
type PersonV1 = { def name: String }
implicit val v1Schema: Schema[PersonV1] = Schema.structural[PersonV1]
```

## How this PR compares to other #519 implementations

There are 6 other open PRs for #519. Here's what makes this one different:

### Serialization - the key requirement others miss

Issue #519 requires migrations to be **pure data that can be serialized**. This PR is the only one that provides actual `Schema` instances for the migration types:

- `Schema[DynamicMigration]`, `Schema[MigrationAction]`, `Schema[DynamicSchemaExpr]` - all provided via `MigrationSchemas`
- Migrations can be round-tripped through JSON (or any other codec) using the existing ZIO Schema codec infrastructure
- #959 explicitly states it **cannot serialize** due to existential types in `SchemaExpr`
- Other PRs provide pure ADTs but no `Schema` instances - serialization is theoretical, not tested

### Compile-time field tracking (Scala 3)

`TrackedMigrationBuilder` uses type-level Tuple accumulation to track which fields have been handled:
- `addField(_.field, default)` adds the target field name to a type-level tuple
- `dropField(_.field)` adds the source field name to a type-level tuple
- `renameField(_.from, _.to)` adds both
- At `.build` time, `MigrationComplete` evidence is required - a macro checks all fields are covered
- Incomplete migrations produce helpful compile errors listing missing fields with hints

### Structural validation via simulation

`MigrationValidator` (857 lines) simulates every action against the source/target schemas at build time:
- Catches field type mismatches, optionality errors, missing fields, wrong collection types
- Goes beyond field-name-presence checking - actually verifies structural compatibility
- No other PR includes this level of build-time validation

### `Schema.structural` derivation (Scala 3)

Derives `Schema` for refinement types (`{ def name: String }`) and union types, enabling migration between structural and nominal types. No other PR includes this.

### Comprehensive testing and documentation

- **600+ tests** across **13 new test files** (organized by component, not one monolithic file)
- **>= 80% branch coverage** on both Scala 3.3.x and 3.7.x, CI-verified
- Full `docs/reference/migration.md` with API reference, examples, error catalog, and best practices

### Side-by-side

| Capability | This PR | #966 (987Nabil) | #968 (yuvrajangadsingh) | #959 (Nanashi-lab) | #882 (Aqil-Ahmad) |
|-----------|---------|-----------------|-------------------------|--------------------|-------------------|
| Serialization schemas (`Schema[DynamicMigration]`) | **Yes** | No | No | Explicitly can't | No |
| Structural simulation validator | **Yes (857 lines)** | No | No | No | No |
| Expression language | `DynamicSchemaExpr` (594 lines, 12 variants) | — | `MigrationExpr` (315 lines, 9 variants) | — | — |
| Compile-time field tracking | **Yes** (`TrackedMigrationBuilder`) | Yes | Yes (`MigrationComplete`) | Yes | Yes |
| `Schema.structural` derivation | **Yes** | No | No | No | No |
| Selector macros (Scala 2 & 3) | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes |
| Runtime validation (`MigrationValidator`) | **Yes** — simulates actions against schemas at build time | No | No | No | No |
| Default value resolution at build time | **Yes** — `resolveDefaults` resolves `DefaultValue` expressions | No | No | No | No |
| Zero core file modifications | No (minimal changes) | No (modifies SchemaExpr/Optic) | **Yes** | No | No |
| Action variants | 15 | ? | 20 (incl. expr-based variants) | ? | ? |
| Tests | **600+** | 74 | 335 | ? | 1000+ (claimed) |
| JMH Benchmarks | No | No | **Yes** (8 operations) | No | No |
| Documentation | **Yes** (`docs/reference/migration.md`) | No | No | No | No |

### What we touch outside `migration/`

- **`Constructor.scala` / `Deconstructor.scala` / `Matcher.scala`** - Added `Structural*` base classes for `Schema.structural`
- **`JsonBinaryCodecDeriver.scala`** - Fixed a pre-existing bug with `usedRegisters` initialization for recursive records
- **`.scalafmt.conf`** - Added `scala213` dialect override for `scala-2/` directories

## Design decisions

- **`build()`** simulates every action against source/target schemas and rejects structural mismatches. `buildPartial` skips this.
- **`TrackedMigrationBuilder`** wraps `MigrationBuilder` (zero changes to shared code) and adds type-level tracking via `Tuple` types. Scala 3 only.
- **Three build modes**: `build` (compile-time + runtime), `buildChecked` (compile-time only), `buildPartial` (no validation).
- **`DefaultValue`** expressions are resolved at build time: strict for forward, best-effort for reverse.
- **Typed `Migration.apply`** is intentionally unsupported for structural-only schemas. Use `applyDynamic`.
- **Scala 2** cannot derive `Schema.structural` or use `TrackedMigrationBuilder` (macro/type system limitations).

## Test coverage

- **13 new test files**, **600+ tests** covering all migration components
- **>= 80% branch coverage** on Scala 3.3.x and 3.7.x (CI-verified)
- All CI jobs pass: lint, testJVM (2.13/3.3/3.7 x JDK 11/25), testJS (2.13/3.3)

## Review guide

**Start here:**
1. `MigrationAction.scala` (305 lines) - the 15-variant action ADT
2. `DynamicMigration.scala` (744 lines) - interpreter over `DynamicValue`
3. `MigrationBuilder.scala` (631 lines) - fluent API + `resolveDefaults` + `build`/`buildPartial`

**Then:**
4. `DynamicSchemaExpr.scala` (594 lines) - expression language + `eval`
5. `MigrationValidator.scala` (857 lines) - structural simulation
6. `TrackedMigrationBuilder.scala` (187 lines) - compile-time field tracking wrapper
7. `MigrationSchemas.scala` (1242 lines) - serialization schemas (skim only)
8. `Selector.scala` + `SelectorMacros.scala` (Scala 2 & 3) - path macros

## Issues

- Addresses #519
- Refs #882, #941
- Replaces #984
title: Schema migration system for ZIO Schema 2

author: jdegoes
body:
## Summary

This PR adds **compile-time resource escape prevention** to the Scope DI library. Resources retrieved from scopes are "locked" using opaque types (`A @@ S`), preventing use-after-close bugs at compile time.

## Key Features

### Scoped Values (`A @@ S`)
- `$[T]` returns `T @@ scope.Tag` — an opaque type that hides all methods on `T`
- Values can only be accessed via the `$` operator: `scoped $ (_.method())`
- The `$` operator requires the matching scope to be in context
- **Scala 3**: Uses opaque types with `Scope[?] { type Tag >: S }` constraint
- **Scala 2**: Uses module pattern + macro to enforce the same constraint at compile time

### Automatic Unscoping
- **`Unscoped` typeclass**: Marks types that can escape freely (primitives, String, collections)
- **`AutoUnscoped` typeclass**: Controls what the `$` operator returns:
  - `Unscoped` types → raw value (e.g., `Int`)
  - Resource types → re-scoped value (e.g., `InputStream @@ Tag`)

### Scope Tag Hierarchy
- Each scope has a path-dependent `Tag` type member
- Child scope tags are supertypes of parent tags (via union in Scala 3)
- Parent-scoped values can be used in child scopes

### For-Comprehension Support
- `map` and `flatMap` on scoped values preserve/widen tags
- `flatMap` correctly handles scope widening with `T >: S`

## API Changes

### New Functions
- `$[T]` — retrieves service as scoped value `T @@ scope.Tag`
- `scoped $ (_.method)` — applies function with scope capability check
- `scoped.map(f)` / `scoped.flatMap(f)` — transforms preserving scope

### Renamed
- `injectedValue(x)` → `injected(x)` — wraps existing value in closeable scope

## Cross-Platform Parity

Both Scala 2.13 and Scala 3.x have identical behavior:
- Same opaque-like semantics (module pattern in Scala 2, opaque types in Scala 3)
- Same `$` operator compile-time safety (macro in Scala 2, refined type in Scala 3)
- Same `Unscoped`/`AutoUnscoped` typeclass hierarchy

## Testing

Comprehensive compile-time safety tests verify code that should NOT compile:
- Cannot call methods on scoped values directly
- Cannot assign scoped value to raw type
- Cannot use `$` with value from different scope
- Cannot use `$` without scope in context
- Resource types cannot escape as raw via `$`

## Zero Overhead

- `@@` is erased at runtime (opaque type alias)
- `scoped`, `map`, `flatMap` are `inline` (Scala 3) / inlined by JIT (Scala 2)
- `$` operator: one virtual call for `AutoUnscoped.apply`

## Documentation

Updated `docs/scope.md` with:
- Resource escape prevention section explaining the problem and solution
- API examples using the `$` operator throughout
- Scala 2 syntax examples
- Scoped Values API reference
title: feat(scope): Add compile-time resource escape prevention with scoped values

author: jdegoes
body:
Adds convenience constructors to make defining Wireable instances more ergonomic:

- `Wireable(value)` - create a Wireable from a pre-existing value
- `Wireable.fromWire(wire)` - create a Wireable from an existing Wire

**Example usage:**
```scala
case class Config(dbUrl: String, port: Int)
object Config {
  given Wireable[Config] = Wireable(Config("jdbc://prod/mydb", 8080))
}

trait Database
object Database {
  given Wireable[Database] = Wireable.fromWire(shared[PostgresDatabase])
}
```

100% statement and branch coverage on Scala 2 and 3.
title: feat(scope): Add Wireable.apply and Wireable.fromWire convenience constructors

author: Godzilla675
body:
## Summary
   - Implement pure, serializable migration core for ZIO Schema 2 (DynamicMigration + MigrationAction + DynamicSchemaExpr schemas).
   - Add Schema.structural derivation for structural records/variants and structural bindings (Scala 3).
   - Add selector macros + builder syntax for optic-style paths (Scala 2/3).
   - Add build-time validation, DefaultValue resolution, correct transform context, and extended runtime path traversal.

   ## Examples

   **Structural schema + addField**
   ```scala
   type PersonV1 = { def name: String }
   @schema case class PersonV2(name: String, age: Int)

   implicit val v1Schema: Schema[PersonV1] = Schema.structural[PersonV1]

   val v1ToV2 =
     MigrationBuilder[PersonV1, PersonV2]
       .addField(_.age, 0)
       .build

  Rename + composition

   @schema case class PersonV3(fullName: String, age: Int)

   val v2ToV3 =
     MigrationBuilder[PersonV2, PersonV3]
       .renameField(_.name, _.fullName)
       .buildPartial

   val v1ToV3 = v1ToV2 ++ v2ToV3
```

  Selector grammar (paths)

   MigrationBuilder[Old, New]
     .dropField(_.address.street)
     .optionalizeField(_.email)
     .buildPartial

  Notable behavior

   - build() validates structural changes (including optionality) by simulating actions.
   - DefaultValue resolves at build time (forward strict; reverse best-effort).
   - Typed Migration.apply returns a MigrationError for structural-only schemas; use applyDynamic.

  Tests

   - sbt -no-colors schemaJVM/test schemaJS/test

  
  Notes / limitations

   - Scala 2 cannot derive Schema.structural for true structural/union types.
   - Typed apply for structural schemas is intentionally unsupported (use applyDynamic).

  Review guide

   - Serialization schemas: schema/shared/src/main/scala/zio/blocks/schema/migration/MigrationSchemas.scala
   - Structural derivation: schema/shared/src/main/scala-3/zio/blocks/schema/SchemaCompanionVersionSpecific.scala (+ bindings)
   - Selector macros + syntax: schema/shared/src/main/scala-2|3/zio/blocks/schema/migration/*
   - Validation: schema/shared/src/main/scala/zio/blocks/schema/migration/MigrationValidator.scala
   - Tests: schema/shared/src/test/scala/zio/blocks/schema/migration/*

  Issues

   - Addresses #519
   - Refs #882, #941
   - /claim #519
title: Schema migration system for ZIO Schema 2

author: jdegoes
body:
## Summary

This PR introduces the **Scope** module - a minimalist dependency injection library that makes **lifecycle errors into compile-time errors**. Most DI libraries verify that your dependencies are wired correctly — Scope goes further by verifying that resources are used within their intended lifecycle.

## Key Features

### Compile-Time Dependency Verification
- Type-level stack tracking ensures you can only access services that exist in scope
- `InStack[T, Stack]` evidence resolved at compile time
- Variance allows `Scope.Has[Dog]` to satisfy `Scope.Has[Animal]`

### Automatic Lifecycle Management
- `AutoCloseable` types automatically have `close()` registered as finalizer
- Manual cleanup via `defer { ... }` for custom resources
- Finalizers run in LIFO order (last registered runs first)
- Parent scope close cascades to children first

### Wire System
- `shared[T]` / `unique[T]` - derive wires that are memoized vs fresh per use
- `Wire(value)` - inject pre-existing values
- `Wireable.from[T]` - enable `injected[T]` for traits with custom construction

### Macro-Based Derivation
- Scala 3: Uses `scala.quoted` macros with Block-based code generation
- Scala 2: Uses whitebox macros with quasiquotes
- Supports **arbitrary constructor arity** (tested up to 20 parameters)
- Analyzes constructor parameters: regular deps, `Scope.Any`, `Scope.Has[Y]`

### Thread-Safe Implementation
- Lock-free `Finalizers` using `AtomicReference` with linked list
- Concurrent `add` and `close` are safe
- `close()` returns `Chunk[Throwable]` - all finalizers run even if some fail

### Error Handling
- `close()` returns `Chunk[Throwable]` containing any finalizer errors
- `closeOrThrow()` convenience method throws first error
- `run` logs errors to stderr; `runWithErrors` captures them explicitly
- `run` can only be called once (prevents double-cleanup bugs)

## API Surface

```scala
// Core types
sealed trait Scope[+Stack]
type Scope.Any = Scope[scala.Any]
type Scope.Has[+T] = Scope[Context[T] :: scala.Any]
trait Scope.Closeable[+Head, +Tail] extends Scope[...] {
  def close(): Chunk[Throwable]
  def closeOrThrow(): Unit
  def run[B](f: Scope.Has[Head] ?=> B): B
  def runWithErrors[B](f: ...): (B, Chunk[Throwable])
}

// Wire derivation
transparent inline def shared[T]: Wire.Shared[?, T]
transparent inline def unique[T]: Wire.Unique[?, T]
def Wire[T](t: T): Wire.Shared[Any, T]

// Usage
inline def injected[T](wires: Wire[?,?]*)(using Scope.Any): Scope.Closeable[T, ?]
def $[T](using Scope.Has[T]): T
def defer(finalizer: => Unit)(using Scope.Any): Unit
```

## Example

```scala
import zio.blocks.scope._

class Config { val dbUrl = "jdbc://localhost" }
class Database(config: Config) extends AutoCloseable {
  def close(): Unit = println("DB closed")
}
class App(db: Database) {
  def run(): Unit = println("Running")
}

@main def main(): Unit =
  Scope.global.injected[App].run {
    $[App].run()
  }
  // Database.close() called automatically
```

## Cross-Platform Support

- **Scala versions**: 2.13.x and 3.x
- **Platforms**: JVM and JavaScript
- 100% statement and branch coverage on all combinations

## Documentation

Comprehensive documentation in `docs/scope.md` including:
- Quick start guide
- Core concepts (Scope, Wire, Wireable)
- Cleanup patterns (AutoCloseable, defer)
- Advanced parameter patterns (Scope.Has[Y], multiple param lists)
- Error handling and thread safety
- Compile-time error messages
- API reference

## Dependencies

- `zio-blocks-chunk` (for `Chunk[Throwable]` error collection)
- `zio-blocks-context` (for `Context`)
- `zio-blocks-typeid` (for type identity)
title: feat(scope): Add Scope module - compile-time verified DI with lifecycle safety

author: plokhotnyuk
body:
These days `scala.BigDecimal` is much handy and safer than `java.math.BigDecimal` that was used in zio-json, no more DoS vulnerability on arithmetic operations (see https://github.com/scala/bug/issues/10882):

```
$ scala-cli
Welcome to Scala 3.7.4 (25.0.1, Java Java HotSpot(TM) 64-Bit Server VM).
Type in expressions for evaluation. Or try :help.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
scala> def timed[A](f: => A): A = { val t = System.nanoTime(); val r = f; println(s"${System.nanoTime() - t} ns"); r }
def timed[A](f: => A): A
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
scala> timed(BigDecimal("1e1000000000", java.math.MathContext.DECIMAL128) + 1)
62149 ns
val res0: BigDecimal = 1.000000000000000000000000000000000E+1000000000
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
scala> timed(BigDecimal("1e1000000000", java.math.MathContext.DECIMAL128) + 1)
45599 ns
val res1: BigDecimal = 1.000000000000000000000000000000000E+1000000000
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
scala> timed(BigDecimal("1e1000000000", java.math.MathContext.DECIMAL128) + 1)
46976 ns
val res2: BigDecimal = 1.000000000000000000000000000000000E+1000000000
```

Also, this PR provides `Json.Number.apply` with `Float` and `Double` arguments to be much faster than corresponding `BigDecimal.apply` from the Scala library.

Results of benchmarks for `Json` parsing and serialization with Scala 3 and JDK 25 on Intel® Core™ Ultra 9 285K CPU @ 3.7GHz (max 5.7GHz, 200S Boost), RAM 64Gb DDR5-6400:

author: yuvrajangadsingh
body:
Implements the schema migration system from #519.

The main design choice here was creating a dedicated `MigrationExpr` type rather than modifying the existing `SchemaExpr`. The reason: `SchemaExpr` contains function-based types (`IsNumeric`, `Optic[_, _, _]`) that aren't serializable, and I didn't want to break or complicate existing code to make them work. A separate `MigrationExpr` keeps everything self-contained — all changes are in the migration package, nothing else is touched.

## How it works

Two-layer architecture following the existing `Patch`/`DynamicPatch` pattern:

- **`DynamicMigration`** — the untyped, serializable core. A vector of `MigrationAction` values (pure ADT, no closures) that operates on `DynamicValue`. Supports composition (`++`), structural reverse, and path-based navigation via `DynamicOptic`.

- **`Migration[A, B]`** — typed wrapper that pairs a `DynamicMigration` with source/target schemas. Handles the conversion between typed values and `DynamicValue`.

- **`MigrationBuilder`** — fluent API using macros to convert `_.field` selector syntax into `DynamicOptic` at compile time (both Scala 2.13 and 3). In Scala 3, there's also a `MigrationComplete` type class that gives you a compile error if your migration doesn't cover all fields.

- **`MigrationExpr`** — pure expression type for computed values: arithmetic, string concat, comparisons, type conversions. All serializable, no closures.

## Actions

20 action types covering records (`AddField`, `DropField`, `Rename`, `TransformValue`, `Mandate`, `Optionalize`, `Nest`, `Unnest`), joins/splits (`Join`, `Split`, `JoinExpr`, `SplitExpr`), type conversions (`ChangeType`, `ChangeTypeExpr`), enums (`RenameCase`, `TransformCase`), and collections (`TransformElements`, `TransformKeys`, `TransformValues`). Plus expression-based variants (`TransformValueExpr`).

Nested migrations work naturally — `TransformCase`, `TransformElements`, etc. contain their own `Vector[MigrationAction]`, so you can express hierarchical transformations.

## Quick example

```scala
// Compile-time validated (Scala 3)
val migration = Migration.builder[UserV1, UserV2]
  .renameField(_.name, _.fullName)
  .addField(_.age, 0)
  .build  // won't compile if fields are missing

// Nest fields into a sub-record
val nest = MigrationAction.Nest(root, "address", Vector("street", "city", "zip"))

// Expression-based transforms
val fullName = field(_.firstName) ++ Literal(" ") ++ field(_.lastName)
```

## Tests

335 tests across 4 spec files, organized the same way the repo does it for `Patch` (separate `PatchSpec`, `DynamicPatchSpec`, etc.):

- **`DynamicMigrationSpec`** — core ops, path navigation, Nest/Unnest, multi-step, laws
- **`MigrationActionSpec`** — action laws, error handling, enum scenarios, reverse round-trips
- **`MigrationBuilderSpec`** — typed `Migration[A, B]`, builder API, composition
- **`MigrationExprSpec`** — expression evaluation, arithmetic, string ops, type conversions

Also added `DynamicMigrationBenchmark` with 8 JMH benchmarks (addField, rename, composed, nested, sequence transform, reverse, nest, unnest) plus a spec that validates benchmark output.

## Why 23 files

12 of the 23 files are platform-specific macro code for Scala 2/3 cross-compilation. The metaprogramming APIs are completely different between `scala.reflect` and `scala.quoted`, so each platform needs its own implementations. This is the same pattern used throughout the repo — `Patch`, `Schema`, `Optic` all have separate platform-specific files per concern.

The remaining 11 are: 5 shared source files (core logic), 4 test files, 2 benchmark files.

Closes #519
title: feat(schema): implement Schema Migration System (#519)

author: 987Nabil
body:
## Summary

Complete migration system with fully serializable expressions and ergonomic builder APIs.

### Key Features

| Feature | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| **DynamicSchemaExpr** | Fully serializable expression ADT (21 case classes) - no Schema[A] embedded |
| **SchemaExpr wrapper** | `SchemaExpr[A, B](dynamic, inputSchema, outputSchema)` for type safety |
| **Nested field tracking** | Dot notation paths (`FieldName["address.street"]`) via intersection types |
| **Migration composition** | `.migrateField(src, tgt, migration)` with explicit/implicit resolution |
| **Ergonomic literal API** | `literal(1000)` with implicit Schema, not verbose DynamicValue construction |
| **MigrationBuilder syntax** | Primary API for all migrations with selector syntax |

### API Examples

**Ergonomic literals:**
```scala
// Before (verbose)
literal(DynamicValue.Primitive(PrimitiveValue.Int(1000)))

// After (ergonomic)  
literal(1000)
```

**MigrationBuilder with selectors:**
```scala
val migration = Migration
  .newBuilder[PersonV1, PersonV2]
  .addField(_.age, literal(0))
  .renameField(_.firstName, _.fullName)
  .build
```

**Nested field migration:**
```scala
val migration = Migration
  .newBuilder[PersonV1, PersonV2]
  .transformNested(_.address, _.address) { builder =>
    builder.addField(_.zip, literal("00000"))
  }
  .build
```

**Migration composition:**
```scala
// Explicit
.migrateField(_.user, _.user, userMigration)

// Implicit - summons Migration[User, UserV2] from scope
.migrateField(_.user, _.user)
```

### Success Criteria Checklist
- [x] `DynamicMigration` fully serializable (no closures, all data)
- [x] `Migration[A, B]` wraps schemas and actions
- [x] All actions path-based via `DynamicOptic`
- [x] User API uses selector functions (`S => A`) for "optics" on old and new types
- [x] Macro validation in `.build` to confirm "old" has been migrated to "new"
- [x] `.buildPartial` supported
- [x] Structural reverse implemented
- [x] Identity & associativity laws hold
- [x] Enum rename / transform supported
- [x] Errors include path information
- [x] Comprehensive tests (191 tests)
- [x] Scala 2.13 and Scala 3.x supported
- [x] **Type-level field tracking with intersection types** (unified Scala 2/3 API)
- [x] **88.53% statement coverage / 84.34% branch coverage**

### Test Coverage

- **191 tests** in MigrationSpec
- **88.53% statement coverage** / **84.34% branch coverage**
- Nested type migration tests (2-4 level nesting)
- Error message tests with explicit assertions
- Only 1 test uses `Migration.fromActions` (as API proof)

### Key Components

| Component | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `DynamicMigration` | Untyped interpreter: applies `MigrationAction`s to `DynamicValue` |
| `Migration[A, B]` | Typed wrapper with `apply` / `applyDynamic` / `reverse` / `++` |
| `MigrationAction` | 17-variant ADT (AddField, DropField, Rename, TransformValue, TransformNested, ApplyMigration, ...) |
| `DynamicSchemaExpr` | Serializable expression language (Literal, Select, Arithmetic, StringOps, ...) |
| `SchemaExpr[A, B]` | Typed wrapper around DynamicSchemaExpr |
| `MigrationBuilder` | Fluent builder with `build` (validates) / `buildPartial` (skips validation) |
| `Selector` + macros | Compile-time selector lambdas (`_.address.street`) to `DynamicOptic` paths |

### Files Changed

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `DynamicSchemaExpr.scala` | Serializable expression ADT |
| `SchemaExpr.scala` | Typed wrapper |
| `MigrationAction.scala` | Added TransformNested, ApplyMigration |
| `DynamicMigration.scala` | Execute new actions |
| `MigrationBuilder.scala` (Scala 2 & 3) | transformNested, migrateField |
| `MigrationSpec.scala` | Comprehensive tests (191) |

Closes #519
title: feat(schema): implement Schema Migration System (#519)

## Summary

Restores structural type schema derivation for both Scala 2 and Scala 3 that was accidentally removed in PR #800 (TypeId migration). Pure structural types like `{ def name: String }` can now derive schemas again.

## Changes

### Scala 3 (`ecda17cb`)
- Added `isStructuralType()` - detects refinement types
- Added `getStructuralMembers()` - extracts field names and types  
- Added `structuralFieldOffset()` - calculates register offsets
- Added `deriveSchemaForStructuralType()` - entry point with JVM reflection check
- Added `deriveSchemaForPureStructuralType()` - full schema generation
- Added structural type branch in `deriveSchema()` before `isNonAbstractScalaClass`
- Enabled PureStructuralTypeSpec tests (4 tests)

### Scala 2 (`fd22f1bd`)
- Added `RefinedType` handling to Scala 2 TypeIdMacros for structural types
- Added structural type schema derivation to Scala 2 SchemaCompanionVersionSpecific
- Added 4 structural type tests mirroring Scala 3 PureStructuralTypeSpec

## Notes

Structural types require JVM reflection for deconstruction via `getMethod`/`invoke`.

Cherry-picked from `perf/typeid-macro-caching` branch to allow independent review/merge.

author: elskow
body:
Implements #519
/claim #519

## Highlight: Hierarchical Tree vs Flat Action List

The spec proposes flat `Vector[MigrationAction]` where each action carries its path:

```scala
// Spec approach
case class DynamicMigration(actions: Vector[MigrationAction])
case class AddField(at: DynamicOptic, default: SchemaExpr[?]) extends MigrationAction
```

Per JDG feedback:
> "nested migrations can't be flat, must be lists of lists where top level encodes depth"

This implementation uses a **recursive tree**:

```scala
sealed trait MigrationStep {
  case class Record(
    fieldActions: Vector[FieldAction],       // Actions at this level
    nestedFields: Map[String, MigrationStep] // Recursive children
  )
}
```

## CompTime Val via Phantom Types

```scala
// Phantom types encode field operations
sealed trait FieldTree
sealed trait Empty extends FieldTree
sealed trait Branch[Name <: String, Op <: Operation, Children <: FieldTree, Siblings <: FieldTree] extends FieldTree

// Builder carries phantom type parameters
final case class MigrationBuilder[A, B, Handled, Provided](...)
```

Each `addTyped`/`dropTyped` call accumulates into the phantom type. At `buildTyped`, a macro compares these against the actual schema diff:

```scala
TypedMigrationBuilderMacro.from[PersonV1].to[PersonV2]
  .addTyped(_.address.zip, DynamicValue.string("00000"))
  .buildTyped  // Compile error if nested field missing
```
**Nb:** Macro files are excluded from coverage in `build.sbt` since macro code executes at comptime and cannot be instrumented for runtime coverage.

## Other Diff

| Spec | Implementation | Justification |
|------|----------------|---------------|
| `SchemaExpr[A, ?]` | `DynamicValueTransform` + `PrimitiveConversion` | Separate value transforms from type conversions |
| Actions carry `at: DynamicOptic` | Tree structure encodes path implicitly | Cleaner nested validation, natural recursion for `reverse` |
title: Migration System for ZIO Schema

author: rajim59

Hi, I have completed the schema migration module following the mentor's requirements.

Features Implemented:

All tests are passing with full coverage. Please review.
title: feat: Implement pure schema migration system

author: Nanashi-lab
body:
/claim #519
/closes #519 

## Compile Time Validation

Both Scala 2 and Scala 3 have compile time validation. They extract the `ShapeTree` of SchemaA and SchemaB, and do a `Tree.diff` which returns added and removed,  of the form  `List[List[Segement]]` 

### Segement

```scala
private[migration] object Segment {
  case class Field(name: String) extends Segment
  case class Case(name: String)  extends Segment
  case object Element            extends Segment
  case object Key                extends Segment
  case object Value              extends Segment
  case object Wrapped            extends Segment
}
```
When `.build` is run, as we go through MigrationAction, each migration action collects `Handled` and `Provided`, which is also of the form `List[List[Segement]] `, which are  then compared against added and removed. On success we compile. 

Example of `List[List[Segement]] `: `List((Field("address"), Field("city")), Field("age"))` -> It has two path `address.city` and `age`. 

Note - This is a different List of Lists, rather than encoding level and field. I though having a clear path to the field which was added or removed was a better decision. 

### Selector Syntax

Support for Selector Syntax for `_.field`, `_.nested.field`, `_.case.when[Batman]`. `_.field.each`. Also includes support for bare Literal SchemaExpr, so it is easier to write migration code. 

## Deviations

### Joint and Split for nested fields. 

` _.address.street + _.address.city → _.address.fullAddress` -> this is done

`_.address.street + _.origin.country → _.address.fullAddress` -> This gets complex since now we have to extract values from multiple nested source paths, combine to targeted nested path, remove the nested source fields, and rebuilt the whole structure immutability.

So if the parent is not the same, it gracefully errors out at both runtime (if `.buildpartial` was used) and during compile validation (if `.build` was used)

### Serialization
Added Serialization - Using a new `DynamicSchemaExpr` a serializable version of `SchemaExpr` . Manually derived schema for all things which make up `DynamicMigration`
```scala 
final case class SchemaExpr[A, B](
  dynamic: DynamicSchemaExpr,
  inputSchema: Schema[A],
  outputSchema: Schema[B]
)
```
### Structural Support
Added Structural tests, to showcase the structural can be migrated properly. Deviation is they are not supported via compile time validation. During `.build` they fall through and are parsed as a `PrimitiveNode`. All the tests currently used `.buildPartial`. Maybe fixable by adding a check to see if they are refinement type and exploring their structure at compile time in both 2 and 3.
title: Schema Migration for zio-blocks

author: jdegoes
body:
## Summary

Refactored TypeRegistry into a composable BindingResolver trait hierarchy with the following changes:

### New API
- **BindingResolver trait**: Read-only interface with `resolve*` methods for all binding types (Record, Variant, Primitive, Wrapper, Dynamic, Seq, Map)
- **++ operator**: Left-biased composition for fallback resolution chains
- **BindingResolver.Registry**: Immutable map-backed resolver with `bind` methods
- **BindingResolver.reflection**: Derives Record bindings for case classes via runtime reflection (JVM only)
- **BindingResolver.defaults**: Pre-configured bindings for primitives, java.time types, and common collections

### Key Improvements
1. **Composable resolvers**: Custom bindings can be combined with defaults and reflection using `++`
2. **Reflection-based derivation**: `BindingResolver.reflection` can automatically derive Record bindings for case classes at runtime
3. **Proper register layout**: The reflection binding uses the correct register types (int, long, float, etc.) matching how the macro generates bindings, ensuring JSON codec roundtrip works correctly
4. **Phantom type flexibility**: `bind[C[_]]` and `bind[M[_,_]]` now accept existential element types since element/key/value types are phantom in `Binding.Seq`/`Binding.Map`
5. **Cross-platform support**: Platform-specific implementations for JVM (full reflection) and JS (no-op)

### Breaking Changes
- Removed `TypeRegistry` object (no backward compatibility alias)
- Renamed `TypeRegistry.default` → `BindingResolver.defaults`
- Renamed `TypeRegistry.empty` → `BindingResolver.empty`

### Tests
- All TypeRegistrySpec tests merged into BindingResolverSpec
- Added tests for reflection-based binding with JSON codec roundtrip
- Reflection-specific tests are JVM-only (`@@ TestAspect.jvmOnly`)
- All tests pass on Scala 2.13 and Scala 3 across JVM and JS
title: Refactor TypeRegistry into composable BindingResolver trait hierarchy

author: 987Nabil
body:
## Summary

This PR fixes regressions introduced by PR #800 (TypeId migration):

1. **TypeIdMacros caching** - Fixes compile time regression (Issue #943)
2. **Structural type derivation (Scala 3)** - Restores accidentally removed structural type support
3. **Structural type derivation (Scala 2)** - Adds structural type support that was missing after migration

## Change 1: Macro Caching for TypeIdMacros (Scala 3)

Implements memoization for TypeIdMacros following @jdegoes' guidance from #943.

### Why Scala 3 Only?

The Scala compiler **does not cache macro expansions** - confirmed by Sébastien Doeraene (Scala 3 core developer). Macros are treated as arbitrary functions, so any caching must be implemented within the macro itself.

Scala 2 macros have a different architecture and didn't show the same regression. Schema derivation also has a different pattern of macro calls that wasn't as affected.

### Implementation

Per @jdegoes' suggestion, the implementation uses **ToExpr type class instances** instead of a parallel data hierarchy:

- Added `given ToExpr[Owner]`, `given ToExpr[TypeParam]`, `given ToExpr[TypeDefKind]`, `given ToExpr[TypeRepr]`, `given ToExpr[Annotation]`, etc.
- Caches store actual runtime types: `ConcurrentHashMap[String, Owner]`, etc.
- Cached build functions use `Expr(value)` with ToExpr to convert to Expr

### What's Cached vs Not Cached

**Cached** (symbol-level metadata):
- Owner path analysis
- Type parameter analysis
- Def-kind classification (class/trait/enum/object/etc)
- Annotation extraction

**NOT cached** (intentionally):
- `Implicits.search()` - runs every invocation to respect user-provided `given TypeId[T]` instances

## Change 2: Restore Structural Type Derivation (Scala 3)

Restores structural type schema derivation that was accidentally removed in PR #800.

### Changes:
- Added `isStructuralType()` - detects refinement types
- Added `getStructuralMembers()` - extracts field names and types
- Added `structuralFieldOffset()` - calculates register offsets
- Added `deriveSchemaForStructuralType()` - entry point with JVM reflection check
- Added `deriveSchemaForPureStructuralType()` - full schema generation
- Added structural type branch in `deriveSchema()` before `isNonAbstractScalaClass`
- Enabled PureStructuralTypeSpec tests (4 tests)

## Change 3: Add Structural Type Derivation (Scala 2)

Adds structural type support to Scala 2, which was missing after the TypeId migration.

### Changes:
- Added `RefinedType` handling to Scala 2 TypeIdMacros for structural types
- Added `isStructuralType()` - detects refinement types with structural members
- Added `getStructuralMembers()` - extracts member names and types
- Added `structuralRegisterKind()` - determines register type for primitives vs objects
- Added `deriveSchemaForStructuralType()` - full schema derivation with JVM-only gate
- Added structural type check in `deriveSchema()` type ladder BEFORE `isNonAbstractScalaClass`
- Added 4 structural type tests mirroring Scala 3 PureStructuralTypeSpec

Structural types require JVM reflection for deconstruction via `getMethod`/`invoke`.

## Test Results

| Test Suite | Result |
|------------|--------|
| `typeidJVM/test` (Scala 3) | ✅ 506 tests passed |
| `schemaJVM/test` (Scala 3) | ✅ 6726 tests passed |
| `typeidJVM/test` (Scala 2) | ✅ 451 tests passed |
| `schemaJVM/test` (Scala 2) | ✅ 6444 tests passed |

## References

- Fixes #943 (compile time regression)
- Based on research from jdegoes' comment: https://github.com/zio/zio-blocks/issues/943#issuecomment-3831041095
title: perf(typeid): macro caching + restore structural type derivation

author: 987Nabil
body:
## Summary

Closes #949

Adds a new `combinators` module providing foundational typeclasses for tuple and sum type operations. These are zero-dependency building blocks for ZIO Blocks.

## Typeclasses

### Combiner[L, R]
Bidirectional tuple flattening with `combine()` and `separate()`.

```scala
val c = Combiner[Int, String]
val combined = c.combine(42, "hello")  // (42, "hello")
val (num, str) = c.separate(combined)  // recovers original parts

// Flattening
val nested = Combiner[(Int, String), Boolean]
nested.combine((1, "a"), true)   // (1, "a", true) - flattened!
```

### Zippable[A, B]
One-way tuple flattening with `zip()` and discard flags. Supports ZIO-style `*>`, `<*`, `<*>` operators.

```scala
val z = Zippable[Int, String]
z.zip(42, "hello")  // (42, "hello")

// Discard semantics
val zRight = Zippable[Unit, String]  // leftIsDiscarded = true
zRight.zip((), "result")  // "result" (Unit discarded)
```

### EitherAlternator[L, R]
Either-based alternation for sum types. Works cross-version (Scala 2 + 3). Allows same-type combinations since `Left`/`Right` provide positional distinction.

```scala
val alt = EitherAlternator[Int, String]
alt.left(42)    // Left(42)
alt.right("x")  // Right("x")

// Same types allowed (unlike UnionAlternator)
val sameType = EitherAlternator[Int, Int]
sameType.left(1)   // Left(1)
sameType.right(2)  // Right(2)
```

### UnionAlternator[L, R] (Scala 3 only)
Union type alternation (`L | R`). Rejects same-type combinations since `A | A` = `A`.

```scala
val alt = UnionAlternator[Int, String]
alt.left(42)    // 42: Int | String
alt.right("x")  // "x": Int | String
```

### StructuralCombiner[A, B] (JVM only, Scala 3 only)
Macro-based structural type merging for combining record-like types.

## Module Structure

```
combinators/
├── jvm/src/main/scala-3/         # StructuralCombiner (JVM-only macro)
├── shared/src/main/scala-2/      # Scala 2 implementations
├── shared/src/main/scala-3/      # Scala 3 implementations  
└── shared/src/test/              # Cross-version tests
```

## Files Added

| File | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `Combiner.scala` | Bidirectional tuple flattening (Scala 2 + 3) |
| `Zippable.scala` | One-way tuple zipping with discard flags |
| `EitherAlternator.scala` | Either-based sum type alternation |
| `UnionAlternator.scala` | Union type alternation (Scala 3 only) |
| `StructuralCombiner.scala` | Structural type merging macro (JVM only) |
| `*Spec.scala` | Comprehensive test suites |
| `CombinatorExamples.scala` | Usage examples |

## Verification

- ✅ Scala 3.7.4: 85 tests passed
- ✅ Scala 2.13.18: 69 tests passed
- ✅ Cross-platform: JVM, JS, Native
title: feat(combinators): add tuple combinators microlib

author: elskow
body:
this implementation uses a hierarchical structure in `MigrationStep.Record`:

```scala
final case class Record(
  fieldActions: Vector[FieldAction],           // Bottom level: add/remove/update at THIS depth
  nestedFields: Map[String, MigrationStep]     // Top level: how deep (recursion)
)
```

Sample for migrating nested structures:

```scala
// Source: Person(name, address: Address(street, city, zip))
// Target: Person(name, address: Address(street, city, postalCode), age)

Record(
  fieldActions = Vector(Add("age", default)),     // depth 0: Person level
  nestedFields = Map(
    "address" -> Record(
      fieldActions = Vector(Rename("zip", "postalCode")),  // depth 1: Address level
      nestedFields = Map.empty
    )
  )
)
```

## API Examples

**String-based API (Scala 2 & 3):**
```scala
MigrationBuilder.from[PersonV1].to[PersonV2]
  .renameField("name", "fullName")
  .addField("email", DynamicValue.Primitive("unknown", StandardType.StringType))
  .build
```

**Selector-based API (Scala 3):**
```scala
MigrationBuilder.from[PersonV1].to[PersonV2]
  .rename(_.name, _.fullName)
  .add(_.email, DynamicValue.Primitive("unknown", StandardType.StringType))
  .buildValidated
```

**Nested paths:**
```scala
MigrationBuilder.from[OrderV1].to[OrderV2]
  .addField("customer.loyaltyPoints", default)
  .renameField("customer.address.zip", "customer.address.postalCode")
  .build
```

Closes #519
/claim #519
title: Algebraic Migration System

author: natinew77-creator
body:
Implements the Schema Migration System for ZIO Schema 2, enabling pure, algebraic schema evolution where migrations are first-class, serializable data with compile-time validation.

- DynamicMigration ADT with MigrationAction operations (AddField, DropField, Rename, Join, Split, Transform)
- MigrationBuilder with SchemaExpr-first API and selector macros for type-safe path extraction
- TrackedMigrationBuilder with ValidationProof for true compile-time validation via `.build`
- DynamicMigrationInterpreter for applying migrations to DynamicValue
- Full reversibility with Resolved.inverse for automatic migration reversal
- PrimitiveConversions with 67 tested safe type coercions
- Nested migration support via DynamicOptic paths
- StructuralMigrationSpec - 36 new structural type tests 
- Complete serialization: Schema[DynamicMigration] and Schema[MigrationAction]
- 1612+ tests covering all operations, laws, and edge cases

Fixes #519
/claim #519
title: feat(migration): Pure algebraic schema migration system

author: yuvrajangadsingh
body:
## Summary

Complete implementation of the Schema Migration System for ZIO Schema 2 as specified in #519.

### Key Features

- **Type-safe Migration Builder** (`TypedMigrationBuilder[A, B]`) with selector-based API
- **Selector expressions**: `_.name`, `_.address.street`, `_.items.each`, `_.country.when[UK]`
- **DynamicOptic hidden from public API** - only exposed internally
- **Full Scala 2.13 and Scala 3.5+ cross-compilation**
- **Comprehensive test coverage**: 271 tests passing on both versions

### Success Criteria Status

| Criterion | Status |
|-----------|--------|
| `DynamicMigration` fully serializable | ✅ |
| `Migration[A, B]` wraps schemas and actions | ✅ |
| All actions path-based via `DynamicOptic` | ✅ |
| User API uses selector functions (`S => A`) | ✅ |
| `.buildPartial` supported | ✅ |
| Structural reverse implemented | ✅ |
| Identity & associativity laws hold | ✅ |
| Enum rename / transform supported | ✅ |
| Errors include path information | ✅ |
| Comprehensive tests | ✅ |
| Scala 2.13 and Scala 3.5+ supported | ✅ |

### Implementation Highlights

1. **Nested field operations** work with direct selectors:
   ```scala
   Migration.typed[WithAddressV0, WithAddressV1]
     .renameField(_.address.street, _.address.streetName)
     .addField(_.address.zipCode, "00000")
     .build
   ```

2. **Collection traversal** via `transformElements`:
   ```scala
   .transformElements(_.tags, ResolvedExpr.Convert("string", "string"))
   ```

3. **Variant case operations** via `renameCase`, `transformCase`:
   ```scala
   .renameCase("UK", "UnitedKingdom")
   ```

4. **Build validation** (runtime) ensures all fields are handled:
   - `build` - throws if incomplete
   - `buildValidated` - returns `Either`
   - `buildPartial` - skips validation

### Files Changed

- `TypedMigrationBuilder.scala` (Scala 2 & 3 versions with platform-specific macros)
- `SelectorMacros.scala` (Scala 2 & 3 versions)
- `MigrationBuilderMacros.scala` (Scala 3 path extraction)
- `DynamicMigration.scala` - enhanced action execution
- `MigrationAction.scala` - complete ADT with all variants
- `MigrationSpec.scala` - comprehensive tests

## Test plan

- [x] All 271 MigrationSpec tests pass on Scala 3 JVM
- [x] All 271 MigrationSpec tests pass on Scala 2.13 JVM
- [x] Nested field operations work correctly
- [x] Collection and variant type operations work
- [x] Build validation catches incomplete migrations
title: feat: Schema Migration System for ZIO Schema 2

author: jdegoes
body:
## Summary

This PR adds two new methods to `TypeId` for JVM reflection support:

- **`TypeId#clazz: Option[Class[_]]`** - Returns the `Class` associated with a nominal `TypeId` on JVM, `None` on JS/Native
- **`TypeId#construct(args: Chunk[AnyRef]): Either[String, Any]`** - Constructs instances using reflection on JVM, returns `Left` error on JS/Native

## Implementation Details

- Platform-specific trait `TypeIdPlatformSpecific` declares the methods, `TypeId` extends it
- `TypeIdPlatformMethods` object in jvm/js/native directories provides platform implementations  
- JVM implementation uses hardcoded constructors for known types (collections, java.time) and reflection for user-defined types
- JS/Native return `None`/`Left` respectively with error messages about JVM-only support
- Used `@annotation.unused` on JS/Native construct params to avoid `-Werror` warnings

## Testing

- Tests use `TestAspect.jvmOnly` to skip construct/clazz tests on JS/Native
- 100% statement and branch coverage achieved on JVM
- Tests cover: primitives, collections, java.time types, user-defined case classes, nested classes, error cases
title: Add TypeId#clazz and TypeId#construct for JVM reflection support

author: jdegoes
body:
## Summary

`Context[+R]` is a covariant, type-indexed heterogeneous collection that stores values indexed by their types with compile-time type safety for lookups.

## Key Features

- **Covariant (+R)** like ZEnvironment for flexible subtyping
- **IsNominalType[A]** typeclass (macro-derived) ensures only nominal types can be stored/retrieved - rejects intersection, union, structural types at compile time
- **IsNominalIntersection[A]** typeclass for decomposing intersection types in prune
- **Platform-specific caching**: JVM uses ConcurrentHashMap, JS/Native use mutable HashMap
- **Supertype lookups**: store `Dog`, retrieve as `Animal`

## API

| Method | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `Context.apply` | Overloaded constructors for 1-10 values |
| `get[A >: R]` | Retrieves value with compile-time guarantee of presence |
| `getOption[A]` | Optional lookup without type bound |
| `add[A]` | Adds value, returns `Context[R & A]` |
| `update[A >: R]` | Transforms existing value in place |
| `++` | Union of two contexts (right wins on conflict) |
| `prune[A >: R]` | Narrows to subset of types |

## Changes to TypeId

Adds `TypeId.erased` method directly on the `TypeId` trait (replaces extension method) for obtaining type-erased keys for use in type-indexed maps.

## Cross-compilation

- Scala 2.13.x and 3.x
- JVM, JS, Native platforms
title: Add Context microlibrary: type-indexed heterogeneous collection

author: yuvrajangadsingh
body:
## Summary

This PR implements a pure, algebraic migration system for ZIO Schema that represents structural transformations between schema versions as first-class, serializable data. 

**This is an improved version of PR #921, addressing the feedback from @jdegoes about compile-time field tracking and nested migration support.**

## Key Features

- **Compile-Time Field Tracking**: Uses phantom type parameters to track which fields have been handled
- **Type-Safe Field Selectors**: `select[T](_.field)` extracts field names as singleton string types
- **NESTED MIGRATION SUPPORT** - Full support for nested structure migrations
- **Build-time Validation**: `build()` only compiles when all source fields are handled and all target fields are provided
- **Fully Serializable**: DynamicMigration can be stored in registries, databases, or files
- **Bidirectional**: Every migration has a structural reverse

## API Overview

### Type-Safe Flat Migrations
```scala
val migration = TypedMigrationBuilder[PersonV1, PersonV2]
  .renameField(select(_.name), select(_.fullName))
  .keepField(select(_.age))
  .addFieldString(select(_.email), "unknown@example.com")
  .build  // Only compiles when all fields are handled!
```

### Nested Migrations
```scala
// Migrate Company with nested Address transformation
val migration = TypedMigrationBuilder[CompanyV1, CompanyV2]
  .keepField(select(_.name))
  .atFieldTransform(select(_.address), select(_.address)) { nested =>
    nested
      .renameField("street", "streetName")
      .addFieldString("zipCode", "00000")
  }
  .build

// Deeply nested (3+ levels) also supported:
TypedMigrationBuilder[DepartmentV1, DepartmentV2]
  .keepField(select(_.name))
  .atFieldTransform(select(_.manager), select(_.manager)) { emp =>
    emp.atField("contact")(contact =>
      contact.addFieldString("fax", "000-000-0000")
    )
  }
  .build
```

## Components Added

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `FieldSet.scala` | Match types for type-level field set operations (Contains, Remove, IsEmpty) |
| `FieldSelector.scala` | Type-safe field selection with singleton string types |
| `SelectorMacros.scala` | Macros for `select[T](_.field)` syntax |
| `TypedMigrationBuilder.scala` | Main builder with compile-time field tracking |
| `TypedMigrationBuilderSpec.scala` | 14 comprehensive tests including nested migrations |

## What Changed from PR #921

This version adds:
1. **Type-safe field selectors** via `select(_.field)` macro
2. **Compile-time field tracking** using tuples of singleton strings
3. **Nested migration support** via `atField`, `atFieldTransform`, `atFieldTyped`
4. **Proper compile-time validation** that all fields are handled

## Test Coverage

- 14 new tests for TypedMigrationBuilder
- 275 total migration tests passing
- Tests cover: flat migrations, nested migrations (3 levels), composition, bidirectional, introspection

Fixes #519

---
*Previous attempt: #921 (closed)*
title: feat(schema): Add Type-Safe Schema Migration System with Nested Support

author: jdegoes
body:
## Issue
Closes #926

## Summary
Restores Scala 3.3 LTS support by splitting version-specific code and making the `-experimental` flag conditional.

## Changes

### Build Infrastructure
Extended `BuildHelper.scala` with a new `scala-3.X-` pattern for mutually exclusive version ranges:

| Directory | Scala 3.3 | Scala 3.4 | Scala 3.5 | Scala 3.6 | Scala 3.7 |
|-----------|-----------|-----------|-----------|-----------|-----------|
| `scala-3` | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| `scala-3.5-` | ✓ | ✓ | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ |
| `scala-3.5+` | ✗ | ✗ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |

- **`scala-3.X+`** (with +): included for versions >= X
- **`scala-3.X-`** (with -): included for versions < X (mutually exclusive with +)
- **`scala-3.X`** (exact): best match, superseded by higher version dirs

### Compiler Flags
- `-experimental` flag now only added for Scala 3.5+ (where `Symbol.newClass` is available)
- Scala 3.3 LTS compiles without experimental features

### Version-Specific Code Split
Created `StructuralBindingMacros.scala` in two versions:
- **`scala-3.5+/`**: Full implementation using `Symbol.newClass` for `Binding.of` on structural types
- **`scala-3.5-/`**: Compile-time error with helpful message directing users to alternatives

### CI Updates
- Test matrix now includes both Scala 3.3.x (LTS) and 3.7.x explicitly
- Skip scaladoc on JDK 25 + Scala 3 (pre-existing scaladoc bug with newer JDKs)

### What Works on Scala 3.3 LTS
- ✅ `Binding.of[CaseClass]` - all case class bindings
- ✅ `Schema[CaseClass].structural` - converting case class schemas to structural types
- ✅ All other Schema and Binding functionality

### What Requires Scala 3.5+
- ❌ `Binding.of[{def name: String}]` - direct structural type binding derivation (uses `Symbol.newClass`)

## Test Results
- ✅ Scala 3.7.4 JDK 11: All tests pass (including 42 structural binding tests)
- ✅ Scala 3.3.7 JDK 11: All tests pass (structural binding tests correctly excluded)
- ✅ Scala 2.13.18: All tests pass
- ✅ All JS and Native platforms pass
title: Restore Scala 3.3 LTS support with version-specific source directories

author: jdegoes
body:
## Summary

Implements #179 - Type Registry & Associated Machinery for Schema Serialization

This PR adds the foundation for schema rebinding:

### New Files
- `TypeRegistry.scala` - Type-safe map from TypeId to Binding
- `Unapply.scala` - UnapplySeq/UnapplyMap type classes for Scala 2 compatibility
- `RebindTransformer.scala` - Transformer to rebind Reflect.Unbound to Reflect.Bound
- `TypeRegistrySpec.scala` - Comprehensive tests for TypeRegistry
- `DynamicSchemaRebindSpec.scala` - Tests for schema rebinding workflow

### Modified Files
- `TypeId` (Scala 2 & 3) - Added `TypeId.unapplied` method and `classTag` lazy val
- `TypeIdOps` - Added unapplied implementation
- `DynamicSchema` - Added `rebind[A](registry: TypeRegistry): Schema[A]` method
- `Binding.of` macro - Changed return type from `Any` to `Binding[_, A]`; rejects applied collection types with helpful compile errors
- `BindingCompanionVersionSpecific` - Removed dead code (deriveMapBinding, deriveSeqBinding, etc.)
- `SeqConstructor` - Simplified to 4 methods (newBuilder, add, result, empty) with ClassTag
- `Reflect.Sequence` - Added `elemClassTag` helper method
- `Optic` - Added `elemClassTag` to AtBinding, AtIndicesBinding, SeqBinding; fixed unsound ClassTag casts

### Key Features
- `TypeRegistry.empty` and `TypeRegistry.default` (pre-populated with primitives, sequences, maps)
- Unified `bind[A](binding: Binding[_, A])` method that pattern-matches on binding type
- Separate `bindSeq` and `bindMap` for type constructors
- Lookup methods with Scala 2-compatible type class evidence
- RebindException with path and type information
- Sound ClassTag handling for primitive arrays via TypeId.classTag

### API Changes
- `Binding.of[List[Int]]` now fails at compile time with: "Use Binding.of[List] for List types, not Binding.of[List[Int]]"
- Use `Binding.of[List]` for type constructor bindings, `Binding.of[Person]` for concrete types
- SeqConstructor simplified: removed specialized primitive methods, added ClassTag requirement

### Commits
1. Initial TypeRegistry implementation with bind/lookup methods
2. Simplify SeqConstructor: remove specialized primitive methods (~1,768 lines removed)
3. Add TypeId.classTag and fix unsound ClassTag casts in Optic

Closes #179
title: Add TypeRegistry and Schema rebinding (#179)

author: yuvrajangadsingh
body:
## Summary

This PR implements a pure, algebraic migration system for ZIO Schema that represents structural transformations between schema versions as first-class, serializable data.

### Key Features

- **Pure Data Migrations**: All migrations are represented as case classes with no closures, making them fully serializable
- **Hierarchical Actions**: Supports nested migrations via `AtField`, `AtCase`, `AtElements`, enabling migrations like `_.address.street -> _.address.streetName`
- **Bidirectional**: Every migration has a structural reverse
- **Introspectable**: Migration logic can be inspected, transformed, and used to generate DDL, upgraders, or downgraders

### Architecture

- **DynamicMigration**: Untyped, fully serializable core that operates on DynamicValue
- **Migration[A, B]**: Typed wrapper with schemas for compile-time safety
- **MigrationAction**: Hierarchical ADT with "lists of lists" structure for nested migrations (the key innovation per @jdegoes feedback)
- **ResolvedExpr**: Pure expressions for value-level transformations (no closures)
- **MigrationBuilder**: Fluent API for constructing migrations

### Example Usage

```scala
import zio.blocks.schema.migration._

case class PersonV1(firstName: String, lastName: String)
case class PersonV2(fullName: String, age: Int)

val migration = MigrationBuilder[PersonV1, PersonV2]
  .joinFields(Vector("firstName", "lastName"), "fullName",
    ResolvedExpr.concatWith(" ")(
      ResolvedExpr.field("firstName"),
      ResolvedExpr.field("lastName")
    ))
  .addFieldInt("age", 0)
  .build

// Nested migrations
val nestedMigration = MigrationBuilder[WithAddressV1, WithAddressV2]
  .atField("address")(
    _.renameField("street", "streetName")
     .addFieldString("zipCode", "00000")
  )
  .build
```

## Test plan

- [x] All 33 migration tests pass on Scala 3
- [x] All 33 migration tests pass on Scala 2.13
- [x] Existing schema tests still pass
- [ ] CI passes

/attempt #519
/claim #519
title: feat(schema): Add Schema Migration System

author: jdegoes
body:
## Summary

This PR adds convenient syntax extensions for working with values that have a  instance in scope.

## New Extension Methods

When you `import zio.blocks.schema._`, any value `a: A` with an implicit `Schema[A]` gains:

| Method | Return Type | Description |
|--------|-------------|-------------|
| `a.diff(b)` | `Patch[A]` | Compute the difference between two values |
| `a.show` | `String` | Render value via `DynamicValue.toString` |
| `a.toJson` | `Json` | Encode to Json AST |
| `a.toJsonString` | `String` | Encode to JSON string |
| `a.toJsonBytes` | `Array[Byte]` | Encode to UTF-8 bytes |
| `a.applyPatch(patch)` | `A` | Apply a patch (lenient mode) |
| `a.applyPatchStrict(patch)` | `Either[SchemaError, A]` | Apply a patch (strict mode) |

For decoding:

| Method | Return Type | Description |
|--------|-------------|-------------|
| `string.fromJson[A]` | `Either[SchemaError, A]` | Decode from JSON string |
| `bytes.fromJson[A]` | `Either[SchemaError, A]` | Decode from UTF-8 bytes |

## Example Usage

```scala
import zio.blocks.schema._

case class Person(name: String, age: Int)
object Person {
  implicit val schema: Schema[Person] = Schema.derived
}

val p1 = Person("Alice", 30)
val p2 = Person("Alice", 31)

// Diff and patch
val patch = p1.diff(p2)
val updated = p1.applyPatch(patch)  // Person("Alice", 31)

// JSON encoding
val json = p1.toJson           // Json AST
val str = p1.toJsonString      // {"name":"Alice","age":30}
val bytes = p1.toJsonBytes     // UTF-8 bytes

// JSON decoding
val decoded = str.fromJson[Person]  // Right(Person("Alice", 30))

// Show
println(p1.show)  // Renders via DynamicValue.toString
```

## Implementation Details

- Scala 2 uses implicit classes
- Scala 3 uses extension methods
- Both implementations are in version-specific files under `scala-2/` and `scala-3/`
- Mixed into `package object schema` via `SyntaxVersionSpecific` trait

## Testing

- 30 test cases with 100% coverage of the syntax extensions
- Tests cover all methods, roundtrip encoding/decoding, and edge cases
- Tested on both Scala 2.13 and Scala 3
title: Add schema syntax extensions for values with Schema instances

author: jdegoes
body:
## Summary

This PR removes the `wrapperPrimitiveType` field from `Reflect.Wrapper` case class constructor and replaces it with a derived method that extracts the primitive type information from `TypeId`.

## Changes

- Removed `wrapperPrimitiveType` field from `Reflect.Wrapper` case class constructor
- Added `wrapperPrimitiveType` method that derives the primitive type from `TypeId`:
  - Checks if `typeId.isOpaque` and extracts from `typeId.representation`
  - Checks if `typeId.isAlias` and extracts from `typeId.aliasedTo`
  - Returns `None` for nominal types (case class wrappers)
- Updated all call sites to remove the `wrapperPrimitiveType` parameter
- Simplified `asOpaqueType` to just call `withTypeId` (primitive type is now derived from TypeId)
- Updated tests to use `TypeId.opaque` with proper representation for opaque/newtype schemas

## Behavior Changes

- `wrapperPrimitiveType` now returns `Some` only for types with `TypeId.opaque` or `TypeId.alias` that point to primitive types
- `wrapperPrimitiveType` returns `None` for case class wrappers (using `TypeId.nominal` or derived `TypeId.of` for non-opaque types)
- Users defining schemas for opaque types should use `TypeId.opaque(name, owner, representation = TypeRepr.Ref(TypeId.underlyingType))` instead of `TypeId.nominal` to get proper primitive type handling

## Testing

- All existing tests pass on both Scala 2.13 and Scala 3
- Updated NeotypeSupportSpec and ZIOPreludeSupportSpec to use `TypeId.opaque` with proper representation
- SchemaVersionSpecificSpec validates the new behavior (returns `Some` for opaque types, `None` for case class wrappers)
title: Remove wrapperPrimitiveType field from Reflect.Wrapper

author: jdegoes
body:
## Summary

This PR improves the Schema <-> JsonSchema roundtrip by:

1. **Preserving type names** - Type names like `Person`, `Color`, `Shape` are now preserved in roundtrip instead of becoming `DynamicValue`
2. **Adding comprehensive validation roundtrip tests** - 70 tests total (up from 49)

## Changes

### Type Name Preservation

- **JsonSchema.scala**: Added `title` parameter to `obj()` helper method
- **JsonBinaryCodecDeriver.scala**: Emit `title` with TypeId name in `toJsonSchema` for records and all variant types
- **JsonSchemaToReflect.scala**: Read `title` and use `TypeId.nominal` instead of `TypeId.of[DynamicValue]`

Before:
```
record DynamicValue { name: wrapper DynamicValue(String) ... }
variant DynamicValue { | Red | Green | Blue }
```

After:
```
record Person { name: wrapper DynamicValue(String) ... }
variant Color { | Red | Green | Blue }
```

### Comprehensive Validation Tests

Added tests for all primitive type roundtrips:
- Int, Long, BigInt, BigDecimal, Float, Double, Boolean, String

Added tests for JsonSchema validation constraints roundtrip:
- **String**: minLength, maxLength, minLength+maxLength, pattern (including email-like regex)
- **Integer**: minimum, maximum, minimum+maximum, exclusiveMinimum, exclusiveMaximum
- **Number**: minimum, maximum, minimum+maximum

## Testing

- All 6237 schemaJVM tests pass on Scala 3.7.4
- All 70 JsonSchemaToSchemaSpec tests pass on Scala 2.13.18

## Future Work (out of scope for this PR)

- `$defs` support for more robust type name preservation via JSON Schema references
- Custom Schema validations (like `Validation.String.NonEmpty`) emitted to JsonSchema constraints
title: Improve Schema <-> JsonSchema roundtrip fidelity

author: 987Nabil
body:
## Summary

Replaces debug-oriented `toString` implementations on `TypeId`, `TypeRepr`, and `TypeParam` with idiomatic Scala type syntax.

### Before
```scala
TypeId.of[List[Int]].toString  // "TypeId.nominal(scala.collection.immutable.List[A])"
TypeParam("A", 0, Variance.Covariant).toString  // "+A@0"
TypeRepr.Intersection(List(a, b)).toString  // "Intersection(Ref(...), Ref(...))"
```

### After
```scala
TypeId.of[List[Int]].toString  // "List[Int]"
TypeParam("A", 0, Variance.Covariant).toString  // "+A"
TypeRepr.Intersection(List(a, b)).toString  // "A & B"
```

## Changes

- **New**: `TypeIdPrinter.scala` - Central utility for consistent type rendering
- **Updated**: `TypeId.toString` (both Scala 2 and 3) - Delegates to TypeIdPrinter
- **Updated**: `TypeRepr.Intersection.toString` and `TypeRepr.Union.toString` - Uses `&` and `|` syntax
- **Updated**: `TypeParam.toString` - Clean format without index (`+A` instead of `+A@0`)
- **Updated**: Tests to match new format

## Hybrid Naming Logic

| Type | Output |
|------|--------|
| `scala.Int` | `Int` |
| `java.lang.String` | `String` |
| `java.util.UUID` | `java.util.UUID` |
| `com.example.Person` | `Person` |

## Testing

- ✅ TypeId tests pass (Scala 3): 397 tests
- ✅ TypeId tests pass (Scala 2.13): 340 tests
- ✅ Schema tests pass: 6080 tests
title: feat(typeid): Pretty-print TypeId, TypeRepr, and TypeParam as idiomatic Scala

author: jdegoes
body:
## Summary

This PR introduces `ChunkMap`, an order-preserving immutable map backed by `Chunk`, and refactors `JsonSchema` to use it for all ordering-sensitive fields.

### Problem

`JsonSchema` uses `Map` for fields like `properties`, `$defs`, etc., which loses key ordering. This has downstream ramifications—for example, `JsonSchemaToReflect` loses ordering of keys, and tests had to use `.toSet` to mask the ordering problems.

### Solution

1. **ChunkMap** (chunk project): A new order-preserving immutable map implementation
   - Backed by parallel `Chunk[K]` and `Chunk[V]`
   - Implements full `scala.collection.immutable.Map` interface
   - O(n) lookup by default, O(1) via `.indexed` method
   - Positional access: `atIndex(i)`, `keyAtIndex(i)`, `valueAtIndex(i)`
   - Order-independent equality (like standard Map)

2. **JsonSchema refactoring**: All `Map` fields replaced with `ChunkMap`:
   - `$vocabulary`, `$defs`, `properties`, `patternProperties`
   - `dependentSchemas`, `dependentRequired`, `extensions`

### Changes

- `chunk/`: Added `ChunkMap.scala` and `ChunkMapSpec.scala`
- `schema/`: Updated `JsonSchema`, `JsonBinaryCodec`, `JsonBinaryCodecDeriver`, `JsonSchemaToReflect`
- Updated all test files to use `ChunkMap`
- Order-preservation test now verifies actual ordering (not just `.toSet`)

### Testing

- All tests pass on Scala 2.13 and 3.x
- Cross-platform (JVM, JS)
- Downstream modules compile (schema-avro, schema-bson, etc.)
- 92.80% statement coverage, 90.01% branch coverage
title: Add ChunkMap and refactor JsonSchema for order preservation

author: jdegoes
body:
## Summary

This PR makes `Binding.Wrapper.unwrap` failable by changing its signature from `A => B` to `A => Either[SchemaError, B]`, and ensures that `SchemaError` is thrown directly (instead of wrapped in `RuntimeException`) when encoding fails.

## Changes

### Core changes
- **`Binding.Wrapper.unwrap`**: Now returns `Either[SchemaError, B]` instead of `B`
- **`Schema#transform(wrap, unwrap)`**: New method that accepts failable `wrap` and `unwrap` functions (both return `Either`)
- **Existing `transformOrFail` and total `transform`**: Adapted to use failable unwrap internally while maintaining their public API

### Error handling improvements
- **`Reflect.Wrapper.toDynamicValue`**: Throws `SchemaError` directly on unwrap failure
- **`DerivedOptics` (Scala 2/3)**: Throws `SchemaError` for wrap/unwrap failures in Constructor/Deconstructor
- **All codec derivers**: Updated to handle `Either[SchemaError, B]` from unwrap:
  - AvroFormat
  - BsonSchemaCodec + ObjectIdSupport
  - ThriftFormat
  - MessagePackBinaryCodecDeriver
  - ToonBinaryCodecDeriver

### Benefits
- Preserves full `SchemaError` with path information instead of wrapping in information-destroying `RuntimeException`
- Enables validation during both encoding and decoding for wrapper types
- Consistent error handling across all codecs

## Testing
- All existing tests pass (schemaJVM for both Scala 2.13 and 3.x, schemaJS)
- All downstream modules pass (schema-avro, schema-bson, schema-thrift, schema-messagepack, schema-toon)
- New tests added for the failable `transform` method in SchemaSpec.scala
title: Make Binding.Wrapper.unwrap failable and throw SchemaError directly

author: jdegoes
body:
## Summary

This PR implements `JsonSchemaToReflect` to convert `JsonSchema` into structured `Reflect` types instead of falling back to `Reflect.Dynamic`. This enables **lossless round-tripping** of schema information and makes the generated `Schema[Json]` useful for deriving Protobuf, Avro, and other format codecs.

## Problem

Previously, `Schema.fromJsonSchema()` produced a `Reflect.Dynamic` that lost all structural information:

```scala
val schema = Schema.fromJsonSchema(JsonSchema.obj(
  properties = Some(Map("name" -> JsonSchema.string(), "age" -> JsonSchema.integer()))
))
// Result: Reflect.Dynamic - no field information preserved!
```

This made the resulting schema useless for:
- Generating Protobuf/Avro/Thrift codecs (they need field structure)
- Schema evolution and migration
- Cross-format conversion

## Solution

New `JsonSchemaToReflect` with a shape analysis layer that classifies JsonSchema patterns:

| JsonSchema Pattern | Reflect Type |
|-------------------|--------------|
| `type: "string/integer/number/boolean"` | `Reflect.Primitive` (wrapped) |
| Object with `properties` | `Reflect.Record` |
| Object with only `additionalProperties` | `Reflect.Map` |
| Array with `items` | `Reflect.Sequence` |
| Array with `prefixItems` + `items: false` | `Reflect.Record` (tuple) |
| `enum` with string values | `Reflect.Variant` (empty Record cases) |
| `oneOf` with key-discriminated cases | `Reflect.Variant` |
| `oneOf` with field-discriminated cases | `Reflect.Variant` |
| Type union with null / `anyOf` with null | Wrapped inner type |
| Unrecognized patterns | `Reflect.Dynamic` (fallback) |

### Key Features

**Primitives with Validation**
- `minLength`/`maxLength` → `Validation.String.Length`
- `pattern` → `Validation.String.Pattern` (takes precedence when both present)
- `minimum`/`maximum`/`exclusiveMinimum`/`exclusiveMaximum` → `Validation.Numeric.Range`

**Records with Metadata**
- Open records tagged with `Modifier.config("json.closure", "open")` for round-trip fidelity
- Proper `Constructor`/`Deconstructor` using Registers

**Enums**
- `Reflect.Variant` with empty `Reflect.Record` cases
- Passes `isEnumeration` check for correct JSON string serialization

## Test Coverage

- **68 tests** in `JsonSchemaToReflectSpec` covering shape analysis, Reflect generation, validation translation, and codec integration
- **30 tests** in `StructuredSchemaFromJsonSchemaSpec` for structure preservation and behavioral compatibility
- **6080 total tests pass** on Scala 3.3.7
- **5799 total tests pass** on Scala 2.13.18

## Known Limitations (Future Work)

These are intentionally deferred to keep this PR focused:

- **Required fields**: All defined properties are currently treated as required; the `required` set is captured but optional field handling needs `Reflect.Optional` support
- **Closed record enforcement**: The `additionalProperties: false` flag is preserved via modifier but not yet enforced during decoding
- **Field variant discriminator**: Field-discriminated variants (`oneOf` with common `const` field) currently delegate to key-variant encoding; needs discriminator field injection
- **Option semantics**: Nullable types create a trivial wrapper; proper `Some`/`None` distinction needs `Reflect.Optional`

## Breaking Changes

Some existing tests in `SchemaFromJsonSchemaSpec` were updated:
- All defined properties are now required (optional field handling TBD)
- Error messages differ due to `Primitive` vs `Dynamic` type handling

## Files Changed

- `Schema.scala`: Use `JsonSchemaToReflect.toReflect()` instead of `Schema[DynamicValue].reflect`
- `JsonSchemaToReflect.scala`: New 637-line implementation with shape analysis and builders (updated to use `TypeId` after #800 merge)
- `JsonSchemaToReflectSpec.scala`: New 839-line comprehensive test suite
- `StructuredSchemaFromJsonSchemaSpec.scala`: New 379-line structure preservation test suite
title: feat(schema): structure-preserving JsonSchema to Reflect conversion
