author: 1234-ad
body:
## Description

This PR implements BSON (Binary JSON) support for ZIO Blocks Schema, addressing issue #683.

## Overview

BSON is a binary serialization format used by MongoDB and other systems. This implementation provides full encoding/decoding capabilities for Scala types to/from BSON format, following the ZIO Blocks architecture pattern established by the existing Avro format.

## What's Included

### Core Implementation
- ✅ **BsonBinaryCodec**: Abstract codec trait extending `BinaryCodec[A]`
- ✅ **BsonFormat**: Main format implementation with `Deriver` for automatic codec derivation
- ✅ **Package utilities**: Convenience methods for encoding/decoding

### Type Support
- ✅ **All primitive types**: Unit, Boolean, Byte, Short, Int, Long, Float, Double, Char, String
- ✅ **Numeric types**: BigInt, BigDecimal
- ✅ **Java time types**: Instant, Duration, Period, LocalDate, LocalTime, LocalDateTime, ZonedDateTime, OffsetDateTime, OffsetTime, Year, YearMonth, Month, MonthDay, DayOfWeek, ZoneId, ZoneOffset
- ✅ **Standard library types**: UUID, Currency
- ✅ **Collections**: List, Vector, Set, Map
- ✅ **Optional values**: Option
- ✅ **Case classes**: Records with automatic derivation
- ✅ **Sealed traits**: Variants/enums with discriminated union encoding
- ✅ **Nested structures**: Arbitrary nesting depth
- ✅ **Recursive types**: Supported via lazy derivation

### Testing
- ✅ **Comprehensive test suite**: `BsonFormatSpec.scala` with 40+ test cases
- ✅ **Round-trip verification**: All tests verify encode → decode → original value
- ✅ **Edge cases**: Empty collections, special values, large numbers, error handling
- ✅ **Examples**: `BsonExamples.scala` with 7 detailed usage examples

### Documentation
- ✅ **README.md**: Complete user guide with examples and API documentation
- ✅ **IMPLEMENTATION.md**: Technical details and architecture decisions
- ✅ **Inline Scaladoc**: Comprehensive code documentation
- ✅ **Usage examples**: Runnable examples demonstrating all features

## Architecture

This implementation follows the ZIO Blocks pattern:

```scala
object BsonFormat extends BinaryFormat[BsonBinaryCodec](
  "application/bson",
  new Deriver[BsonBinaryCodec] { ... }
)
```

### Key Components

1. **Deriver Pattern**: Automatic codec derivation using `Reflect` types
2. **Binding System**: Type-safe construction/deconstruction with `Registers`
3. **Binary Codec**: Efficient encoding/decoding with BSON readers/writers
4. **Error Handling**: Comprehensive error reporting with `SchemaError`

### Encoding Strategy

- **Records** (case classes) → BSON Documents
- **Variants** (sealed traits) → Documents with `$type` and `$value` fields
- **Sequences** → BSON Arrays
- **Maps** → BSON Documents (keys as field names)
- **Options** → Value or BSON Null
- **Primitives** → Native BSON types

## Usage Example

```scala
import zio.blocks.schema._
import zio.blocks.schema.bson.BsonFormat

case class Person(name: String, age: Int)
object Person {
  given Reflect[BsonFormat.type, Person] = Reflect.derive[BsonFormat.type, Person]
}

val person = Person("Alice", 30)
val codec = summon[BsonFormat.TypeClass[Person]]

// Encode to BSON
val encoded: Array[Byte] = codec.encode(person)

// Decode from BSON
val decoded: Either[SchemaError, Person] = 
  codec.decode(java.nio.ByteBuffer.wrap(encoded))
```

## Build Configuration

Added `schema-bson` module to `build.sbt`:
- Depends on `schema.jvm` for core functionality
- Uses `org.mongodb:bson:5.3.0` for BSON implementation
- Includes test dependencies (zio-test)
- Integrated into build aliases (`testJVM`, `examples`, `benchmarks`)

## Testing

Run tests with:
```bash
sbt schema-bson/test
```

All tests pass with comprehensive coverage:
- Primitive types
- Case classes and nested structures
- Sealed traits and variants
- Collections and maps
- Optional values
- Java time types
- Edge cases and error handling

## Comparison with Old Implementation

### Old ZIO Schema 1.x BSON
- Used `Schema[A]` directly
- Separate `BsonEncoder` and `BsonDecoder` traits
- Configuration-based sum type handling

### New ZIO Blocks BSON
- Uses `Reflect[F, A]` and `Binding`
- Unified `BsonBinaryCodec[A]`
- Follows ZIO Blocks architecture
- Simpler, more consistent API

## Performance

BSON is designed for performance:
- Binary encoding (faster than text-based formats)
- Length prefixes for quick traversal
- Compact representation
- Efficient register-based construction

## Dependencies

Minimal dependencies:
```scala
"org.mongodb" % "bson" % "5.3.0"
```

## Files Changed

### New Files
- `schema-bson/src/main/scala/zio/blocks/schema/bson/BsonBinaryCodec.scala` (195 lines)
- `schema-bson/src/main/scala/zio/blocks/schema/bson/BsonFormat.scala` (850+ lines)
- `schema-bson/src/main/scala/zio/blocks/schema/bson/package.scala` (85 lines)
- `schema-bson/src/test/scala/zio/blocks/schema/bson/BsonFormatSpec.scala` (250+ lines)
- `schema-bson/src/test/scala/zio/blocks/schema/bson/BsonExamples.scala` (200+ lines)
- `schema-bson/README.md` (comprehensive documentation)
- `schema-bson/IMPLEMENTATION.md` (technical details)

### Modified Files
- `build.sbt` (added schema-bson module configuration)

## Checklist

- ✅ Implements all required functionality from issue #683
- ✅ Follows ZIO Blocks architecture patterns
- ✅ Comprehensive test coverage (40+ tests)
- ✅ Complete documentation (README, examples, Scaladoc)
- ✅ Build configuration updated
- ✅ All tests pass
- ✅ Code formatted and linted
- ✅ No breaking changes to existing code

## Related Issues

Closes #683

## Future Enhancements

Potential improvements for future PRs:
- Configuration support for custom field mapping
- Alternative variant encoding strategies
- MongoDB-specific type support (ObjectId as first-class type)
- Performance optimizations (buffer pooling, streaming)
- Schema evolution support

## Notes

This implementation provides a solid foundation for BSON support in ZIO Blocks. It's production-ready, well-tested, and fully documented. The architecture is extensible for future enhancements while maintaining backward compatibility.

Ready for review! 🚀
title: feat: Add BSON support for ZIO Blocks Schema (#683)

author: 1234-ad
body:
## Summary

This PR implements `JsonPatch` for computing diffs and applying patches to JSON values as requested in issue #685. This builds on the `Json` data type from #732 and follows RFC 6902 (JSON Patch) standard.

## Changes

### Core Implementation

**`JsonPatch.scala`** - Complete JSON Patch implementation with:

✅ **RFC 6902 Operations:**
- `add` - Add a value at a path (supports objects and arrays)
- `remove` - Remove a value at a path
- `replace` - Replace a value at a path
- `move` - Move a value from one path to another
- `copy` - Copy a value from one path to another
- `test` - Test that a value equals expected (for conditional patches)

✅ **Diff Computation:**
- Automatic diff generation between two JSON values
- Minimal patch generation (only changed fields)
- Recursive diffing for nested objects and arrays
- Smart handling of additions, removals, and modifications

✅ **Patch Application:**
- Apply patches to JSON values with error handling
- Composable patches (combine multiple patches)
- Detailed error messages for debugging

✅ **RFC 6902 Format:**
- Serialize patches to standard JSON Patch format
- Parse patches from JSON Patch format
- Full roundtrip support

✅ **Schema Integration:**
- Full `Schema[JsonPatch]` instance
- Automatic codec derivation
- Type-safe serialization/deserialization

**`JsonPatchSpec.scala`** - Comprehensive test suite:
- ✅ All 6 operation types tested
- ✅ Diff computation tests
- ✅ Patch application tests
- ✅ RFC 6902 format parsing/serialization
- ✅ Composition tests
- ✅ Error handling tests
- ✅ Edge cases (empty patches, root replacement, etc.)

## Design Decisions

1. **RFC 6902 Compliance** - Full compliance with JSON Patch standard for interoperability
2. **Builds on Json type** - Leverages the Json ADT from #732 for type safety
3. **JsonPath integration** - Uses JsonPath for addressing locations
4. **Composable patches** - Patches can be combined with `++` operator
5. **Error handling** - Returns `Either[String, Json]` with descriptive error messages
6. **Schema integration** - Full Schema support enables automatic codec derivation

## Example Usage

```scala
// Create a patch manually
val patch = JsonPatch(Vector(
  JsonPatch.Operation.Add(JsonPath.root / "name", Json.Str("Alice")),
  JsonPatch.Operation.Replace(JsonPath.root / "age", Json.Num(31))
))

// Apply patch
val json = Json.obj("age" -> Json.Num(30))
val result = patch.apply(json) // Right({"name": "Alice", "age": 31})

// Compute diff automatically
val old = Json.obj("name" -> Json.Str("Alice"), "age" -> Json.Num(30))
val updated = Json.obj("name" -> Json.Str("Bob"), "age" -> Json.Num(30))
val diff = JsonPatch.diff(old, updated)
// Generates: Replace(/name, "Bob")

// RFC 6902 format
val rfc6902 = patch.toJson
// [{"op": "add", "path": "/name", "value": "Alice"}, ...]

val parsed = JsonPatch.fromJson(rfc6902)
// Right(JsonPatch(...))

// Compose patches
val patch1 = JsonPatch.single(Operation.Add(path1, value1))
val patch2 = JsonPatch.single(Operation.Remove(path2))
val combined = patch1 ++ patch2
```

## RFC 6902 Examples

```json
[
  { "op": "add", "path": "/user/name", "value": "Alice" },
  { "op": "remove", "path": "/user/age" },
  { "op": "replace", "path": "/active", "value": true },
  { "op": "move", "from": "/old/path", "path": "/new/path" },
  { "op": "copy", "from": "/source", "path": "/destination" },
  { "op": "test", "path": "/version", "value": "1.0" }
]
```

## Benefits

- 🎯 **RFC 6902 compliant** - Standard JSON Patch format
- 🎯 **Automatic diff generation** - Compute minimal patches
- 🎯 **Type-safe** - Leverages Json ADT for compile-time safety
- 🎯 **Composable** - Combine patches easily
- 🎯 **Well tested** - Comprehensive test coverage
- 🎯 **Schema integration** - Automatic codec derivation
- 🎯 **Error handling** - Descriptive error messages

## Testing

All tests pass locally. The implementation includes:
- Operation tests for all 6 operation types
- Diff computation tests (simple and complex)
- Patch application tests
- RFC 6902 format parsing and serialization
- Composition tests
- Error handling tests
- Edge case tests

## Dependencies

- **Depends on:** #732 (Json data type)
- **Implements:** #685 (JsonPatch) - $1k bounty

## Related Issues

- Depends on #732 (Add Json data type) - $2k bounty
- Implements #685 (Add JsonPatch) - $1k bounty
- **Total bounty value:** $3k

## Checklist

- [x] Implementation complete
- [x] Tests added and passing
- [x] Documentation included (ScalaDoc)
- [x] RFC 6902 compliant
- [x] Schema integration
- [x] All 6 operations implemented
- [x] Diff computation working
- [x] No breaking changes

---

This implementation provides a complete JSON Patch solution for ZIO Blocks, enabling:
- Efficient JSON diffing and patching
- Standard RFC 6902 format support
- Type-safe JSON manipulation
- Integration with existing ZIO Blocks ecosystem

The combination of #732 (Json) and #685 (JsonPatch) provides a powerful foundation for JSON manipulation in ZIO Blocks! 🚀
title: Add JsonPatch for diffing and patching JSON - Implements #685

author: 1234-ad
body:
## Summary

This PR implements a comprehensive `Json` data type for ZIO Blocks as requested in issue #679. This provides the foundation for JsonPatch (#685) and future JsonSchema support.

## Changes

### Core Implementation

**`Json.scala`** - Complete JSON ADT with:
- ✅ All JSON value types: `Null`, `Bool`, `Num`, `Str`, `Arr`, `Obj`
- ✅ Rich navigation API: `\` for fields, `apply` for indices, `at` for paths
- ✅ Manipulation methods: `merge`, `update`, `delete`
- ✅ Transformation methods: `map`, `filter`, `transform`
- ✅ Type-safe accessors: `asString`, `asNumber`, `asBoolean`, `asObject`, `asArray`
- ✅ Type predicates: `isNull`, `isObject`, `isArray`, `isString`, `isNumber`, `isBoolean`
- ✅ Functional operations: `fold`, `mapArray`, `mapObject`
- ✅ DynamicValue integration: bidirectional conversion
- ✅ Serialization: `prettyPrint`, `toCompactString` with proper escaping
- ✅ Schema derivation: Full `Schema[Json]` instance
- ✅ Smart constructors: `fromInt`, `fromString`, `fromBoolean`, `fromOption`, `fromIterable`, `fromMap`

**`JsonPath.scala`** - RFC 6901 JSON Pointer support:
- ✅ Path-based navigation, update, and delete operations
- ✅ Composable path construction with `/` and `apply`
- ✅ JSON Pointer parsing and serialization
- ✅ Proper escaping/unescaping per RFC 6901

**`JsonCursor.scala`** - Functional cursor for JSON navigation:
- ✅ Immutable navigation with history tracking
- ✅ Field and index navigation
- ✅ Replace and delete operations
- ✅ Path preservation across operations

**`JsonSpec.scala`** - Comprehensive test suite:
- ✅ Constructor tests for all JSON types
- ✅ Navigation and path operations
- ✅ Manipulation (merge, update, delete)
- ✅ Transformation (map, filter, transform)
- ✅ Serialization (compact and pretty print)
- ✅ DynamicValue conversion and roundtrip
- ✅ JsonPath and JsonCursor tests
- ✅ Smart constructor tests
- ✅ Fold operation tests

## Design Decisions

1. **BigDecimal for numbers** - Provides arbitrary precision and avoids floating-point issues
2. **Vector for arrays** - Efficient indexed access and immutable updates
3. **Map for objects** - Natural representation with O(log n) lookups
4. **RFC 6901 compliance** - Standard JSON Pointer format for interoperability
5. **DynamicValue integration** - Seamless schema operations and codec derivation
6. **Immutable operations** - All transformations return new instances

## Benefits

- 🎯 **Foundation for JsonPatch** - Provides all primitives needed for #685
- 🎯 **Schema integration** - Full Schema[Json] enables automatic codec derivation
- 🎯 **Type safety** - Compile-time guarantees with runtime flexibility
- 🎯 **Ergonomic API** - Intuitive navigation and manipulation
- 🎯 **RFC compliance** - Standard JSON Pointer support
- 🎯 **Well tested** - Comprehensive test coverage

## Testing

All tests pass locally. The implementation includes:
- Unit tests for all constructors
- Navigation and path operation tests
- Manipulation and transformation tests
- Serialization tests with edge cases
- DynamicValue roundtrip tests
- JsonPath RFC 6901 compliance tests
- JsonCursor functional tests

## Related Issues

- Implements #679 (Add Json data type) - $2k bounty
- Enables #685 (Add JsonPatch) - $1k bounty

## Checklist

- [x] Implementation complete
- [x] Tests added and passing
- [x] Documentation included (ScalaDoc)
- [x] RFC 6901 compliant
- [x] Schema integration
- [x] DynamicValue conversion
- [x] No breaking changes

---

This implementation provides a solid foundation for JSON manipulation in ZIO Blocks and enables the JsonPatch implementation in #685. The API is designed to be intuitive, type-safe, and performant while maintaining full compatibility with the existing ZIO Blocks ecosystem.

author: Nanashi-lab
body:
### Implementation 

I have implemented the port, using zio-bson version **1.0.6**, (compatible with 3.3.x), 1.0.7 and 1.0.8 are only compatible with 3.4.x and 3.5.x respectively.  zio-bson does the most of heavy lifting.

Implemented `ObjectId`  - Since we dont have `TypeId`, it checks for typename `ObjectId` in namespace `org.bson.type`. The ObjectId is represented as a wrapper around String (hex representation), but when encoded to BSON, it uses the native BsonType.OBJECT_ID (12-byte format) via special handling in BsonSchemaCodec.


### Missing from the implementation 
#### BSON Annotation, the tests for it and the mixed annotation priority tests.

zio-bson comes with it own annotation, in old implementation they have priority over zio schema annotation, I have replicated the said annotations, using either `Modifier` or `Config`. 

zio-bson annotations, at `bson/annotation.scala` are Scala annotations classes, and Not `Modifer` instances. `Term` and `Reflect` types only store, `Modifer.Term` and `Modifer.Reflect`, they do not preserve raw scala annotation. (Fairly certain of this)

I tried doing `case class bsonField(name: String) extends Modifier.Term`, but both of those are sealed traits and cannot be extended outside of their source files. I can use Modifer.config for BSON specific behavior 
```scala
case class User(
    @Modifier.config("bson.field", "bson_name")
    @Modifier.rename("json_name")
    field: String
  )
 ```
But it is a very verbose syntax and is not comptable with old `@bsonField` annotation. This means there are not tests for the annotations and no tests for mixed config priority of annotation testing. I am unsure how to handle this or what the expectation here is.

The current implementation - 

 | Feature | Old Schema Annotation | BSON Annotation | Current `zio-blocks` |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Field rename** | `@fieldName("x")` | `@bsonField("y")`  | `Modifier.rename("y")` |
| **Case rename** | `@caseName("x")` | `@bsonHint("y")`  | `Modifier.rename("y")` |
| **Transient field/case** | `@transientField` / `@transientCase` | `@bsonExclude`  | `Modifier.transient()` |
| **Reject extra fields** | `@rejectExtraFields` | `@bsonNoExtraFields`  | `Config.withIgnoreExtraFields(false)` |
| **Discriminator name** | `@discriminatorName("x")` | `@bsonDiscriminator("y")`  | `Config.withSumTypeHandling(DiscriminatorField("y"))` |

/claim #683
/closes #683
title: BSON support for zio-blocks

author: ethanwater
body:
_this is a new PR as the original PR (#715) was rebased and caused the diff to be overly complex: same code- easier to review_

This PR introduces a comprehensive JSON handling system for the ZIO Blocks schema library, enabling seamless serialization, deserialization, and manipulation of JSON data- fully integrating into the existing schema framework. This is a complete implementation for the plan provided in #679. All tests pass and it runs like butter. 🧈

**Video:**               [(Overview + Test Results)](https://imgur.com/a/0bEceiU)
**Test Results (Gist)**: https://gist.github.com/ethanwater/6fa703a0ec39d87c5bae3f4698526f5e

## Overview

The JSON module provides:

- **Encoding/Decoding**: Type class-based `JsonEncoder` and `JsonDecoder` with automatic derivation from schemas and support for explicit codec overrides
- **Type-Safe Navigation**: `JsonSelection` enables fluent chaining of path queries with error accumulation, allowing operations on multiple selected values without immediate error handling
- **Error Tracking**: `JsonError` captures detailed context including path information, byte offsets, and line/column numbers for precise error reporting
- **Merge Strategies**: Configurable merging of JSON values with multiple strategies (Auto, Deep, Shallow, Concat, Replace, Custom)
- **String Interpolators**: Ergonomic Scala 3 and Scala 2 macro-based interpolators for JSON paths (`p"..."`) and literals (`j"..."`)
- **Bridge Layer**: `JsonBridge` provides low-level integration with `JsonReader`/`JsonWriter` for binary codec support

## Core Json Data Model

The Json.scala file implements the complete JSON ADT with comprehensive support for:

### **Type System**
- `Object`: Unordered key-value pair collections
- `Array`: Ordered element sequences
- `String`: Unicode text values
- `Number`: Precision-preserving numeric values (stored as strings)
- `Boolean`: `true`/`false` values
- `Null`: JSON null representation

### **Navigation & Query**
- Path-based access via `DynamicOptic` (e.g., `json.get(p"users[*].name")`)
- Index-based access for arrays
- Key-based access for objects
- Type filtering methods (`asObject`, `asArray`, `asString`, etc.)

### **Mutation Operations**
- `get()`: Select values at paths
- `set()`: Replace values at paths
- `modify()` / `modifyOrFail()`: Transform values using functions
- `insert()`: Add values at paths (creating intermediate structures as needed)
- `delete()` / `deleteOrFail()`: Remove values from paths
- `merge()`: Combine JSON values with configurable strategies

### **Transformations**
- `transformUp()`: Bottom-up recursive transformation with path context
- `transformDown()`: Top-down recursive transformation with path context
- `transformKeys()`: Rename all object keys via a transformation function
- `filter()`: Remove entries matching predicates
- `fold()`: Accumulate values across the JSON structure

### **I/O & Serialization**
- Parsing from strings, byte arrays, `ByteBuffer`, `Reader`, and Chunk
- Encoding to strings, byte arrays, `ByteBuffer`, `Writer`, and Chunk
- Configurable formatting via `WriterConfig` and `ReaderConfig`
- `DynamicValue` interoperability for cross-schema-type conversions
- Typed encoding/decoding via `JsonEncoder`/`JsonDecoder`

### **Comparison & Hashing**
- Deep equality comparison with proper numeric value handling
- Structured hashCode implementation

## Placeholder APIs

The following features are currently stubbed out for future implementation (tracked in #685):

- **JsonPatch**: RFC 6902 JSON patch operations plus LCS-based sequence diffs and string diffs
- **JsonSchema**: JSON Schema validation support

## Architecture

The module leverages ZIO Blocks' Schema and `DynamicOptic` abstractions, allowing JSON operations to participate in the broader schema ecosystem while maintaining flexibility through explicit codec instances when needed.

***notes*** 
I plan to tackle #685 (JsonPatch implementation) next, likely building on top of this branch. I deliberately excluded it from this PR to keep the scope focused and avoid conflating multiple features in a single changeset.

author: isi1314
body:
# ZIO Schema Migration System

Implements a pure, algebraic migration framework for ZIO Schema 2 with macro-validated field selectors.

/claim #519

## 🎯 What's Implemented

### Core Features (100%)
- ✅ Pure, serializable migrations (`DynamicMigration`)
- ✅ Type-safe wrapper (`Migration[A, B]`)
- ✅ Composable migrations (v1 → v2 → v3)
- ✅ Reversible migrations where possible
- ✅ Automatic optimization
- ✅ Comprehensive error handling

### Macro-Validated API (100%) ⭐
- ✅ Type-safe field selectors: `.addField(_.age, 0)`
- ✅ HOAS pattern matching (novel solution)
- ✅ Nested fields: `.addField(_.address.street, "")`
- ✅ Both `_.field` and `p => p.field` syntax
- ✅ Compile-time validation
- ✅ IDE autocomplete support

### Testing (100%)
- ✅ 42/42 tests passing
- ✅ 9 unit tests
- ✅ 27 property-based tests (algebraic laws)
- ✅ 6 macro tests (HOAS validation)

### Documentation (100%)
- ✅ README.md - Comprehensive guide
- ✅ FINAL_STATUS.md - Complete project status
- ✅ MACRO_STATUS.md - Technical deep-dive
- ✅ HOAS_BREAKTHROUGH.md - Discovery story

## 🔑 Key Innovation: HOAS Pattern

Solved Scala 3's eta-expansion problem using Higher-Order Abstract Syntax pattern matching:

\`\`\`scala
selector match {
  case '{ (x: A) => ($f(x): Any) } =>
    // Captures lambda BEFORE eta-expansion!
    extractFromBody(f.asTerm)
}
\`\`\`

This enables true type-safe field selectors without runtime overhead.

## 📊 Statistics

- **Completion**: 92% (only Scala 2.13 cross-compilation missing)
- **Source Files**: 15 Scala files
- **Test Files**: 5 test suites
- **Tests**: 42/42 passing
- **Documentation**: 4 comprehensive markdown files

## 🚀 Quick Start

\`\`\`scala
import zio.schema._
import zio.schema.migration._

case class UserV1(name: String)
case class UserV2(name: String, age: Int)

val migration = MigrationBuilder[UserV1, UserV2]
  .addField(_.age, 18)  // Type-safe!
  .build

val result = migration(UserV1("Alice"))
// Right(UserV2("Alice", 18))
\`\`\`

## 🧪 Running Tests

\`\`\`bash
sbt test
# 42 tests passed. 0 tests failed. 0 tests ignored.
\`\`\`

## 📚 Documentation

See [schema-migration/FINAL_STATUS.md](schema-migration/FINAL_STATUS.md) for complete project status and technical details.

## ⏱️ Development Timeline

- **Total Time**: ~10 hours
- **HOAS Discovery**: 16 minutes (after 70 min compiler plugin attempt)
- **Achievement**: Novel solution to Scala 3 metaprogramming challenge

## 🎯 Production Ready

- ✅ Type-safe migrations
- ✅ Fully tested with property-based verification
- ✅ Clean, maintainable code
- ✅ Comprehensive documentation
- ✅ Working examples
- ✅ Serializable for storage/transmission

## 🎥 Demo Video

Demo video showing the implementation and tests: [full-demo.mp4](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/...)

---

**Ready for review!**

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Akshatthakur22
body:
This PR adds JSON encoding and decoding support derived from `Schema[A]` using
`DynamicValue` as the intermediate representation. #679 

## Summary
- Introduces a minimal `Json` ADT for representing JSON values  
- Adds `JsonEncoder[A]` and `JsonDecoder[A]` typeclasses  
- Provides schema-based derivation following the existing pattern:

    Schema[A]  ⇄  DynamicValue  ⇄  Json

- No Deriver / no reflection / no registers  
- Does **not** modify `Schema` itself  
- Implementation is small, explicit, and consistent with the current codec design

## Design Notes
- DynamicValue is the authority for encoding/decoding; Schema drives correctness
- A small structural heuristic is used when converting JSON → DynamicValue
  (single-field uppercase keys treated as variants).  
  This is validated by `schema.fromDynamicValue`, so incorrect cases fail safely.
- Scope is intentionally limited to codec derivation only (per issue #679)

## Tests
Added focused tests covering:
- Simple and nested case class round-trips  
- Optional fields  
- Failure cases with clear errors  
- All tests pass on JVM and JS

## Status
- Code formatted (`scalafmtCheckAll`)  
- Full test suite passing  
- Ready for maintainer review  

/claim #679
title: Add Json codec derivation via DynamicValue (no Deriver)

This pull request introduces support for JSON Schema generation in the ZIO Blocks schema library. It adds a new `JsonSchemaFormat` module that allows users to derive JSON Schema representations from their Scala types, and updates documentation to reflect this new capability.

**JSON Schema support:**

* Added a new module, `schema-json-schema`, which provides the `JsonSchema` type and related utilities for representing and generating JSON Schema Draft 2020-12 schemas from Scala types. (`schema-json-schema/src/main/scala/zio/blocks/schema/jsonschema/JsonSchema.scala`)
* Implemented the `JsonSchemaFormat` deriver, allowing automatic derivation of JSON Schema from any type that has a ZIO Blocks `Schema`, including handling of primitives, records, variants, sequences, maps, and wrappers. (`schema-json-schema/src/main/scala/zio/blocks/schema/jsonschema/JsonSchemaFormat.scala`)

**Documentation and usage updates:**

* Updated both `README.md` and `docs/index.md` to mention JSON Schema as a supported feature, with usage examples and dependency instructions. author: John6150
body:
/claim https://github.com/zio/zio-blocks/issues/685


What Was Done:

Json.scala
Created a JSON Abstract Data Type with 6 cases (Object, Array, String, Number, Boolean, Null) plus JsonError for error handling. Includes toDynamicValue/fromDynamicValue conversions to bridge with the existing schema system.

JsonPatch.scala
Implemented the core patching engine:

diff(old, new) - Computes minimal operations to transform one JSON into another using LCS algorithms for strings/arrays apply(json, mode) - Applies operations with 3 modes: Strict (fail on error), Lenient (skip errors), Clobber (force changes) ++ - Composes patches sequentially (monoid)
Operations - Set, NumberDelta, StringEdit, ArrayEdit, ObjectEdit cover all JSON mutation types Interop - toDynamicPatch/fromDynamicPatch for converting to/from the existing DynamicPatch type JsonPatchSpec.scala
Property-based and unit tests verifying:

Algebraic laws (monoid identity/associativity, diff roundtrip) All operation types work correctly
Mode behaviors differ appropriately
Edge cases and error handling

How It Solves Issue #685
The issue requested a JSON-specific patching system that mirrors DynamicPatch but is optimized for JSON's simpler model (4 leaf types vs 30 primitive values). This implementation provides exactly that—a complete, lawful, tested JsonPatch type that can diff, apply, and compose patches on JSON values with configurable failure handling.

author: hartmade
body:
## Summary

Port BSON support from ZIO Schema v1 to ZIO Schema 2 in a new top-level `schema-bson` project.

### Implementation includes:
- `BsonBinaryCodec` abstract class extending `BinaryCodec[A]`
- `BsonFormat` object with `Deriver[BsonBinaryCodec]`
- Support for all 30 primitive types (Unit, Boolean, Byte, Short, Int, Long, Float, Double, Char, String, BigInt, BigDecimal, all java.time types, Currency, UUID)
- Record, variant, sequence, map, dynamic value, and wrapper support
- Recursive type support via lazy initialization
- Comprehensive test suite with **135 tests**

### Deriver Methods Implemented:
- `derivePrimitive` - All primitive types
- `deriveRecord` - Case classes encoded as BSON documents
- `deriveVariant` - Sealed traits with discriminator field
- `deriveSequence` - Collections encoded as BSON arrays
- `deriveMap` - Maps encoded as BSON documents (string keys) or arrays (non-string keys)
- `deriveDynamic` - DynamicValue support
- `deriveWrapper` - Wrapper types with validation

### Dependencies:
- `org.mongodb:bson:5.2.0`

## Test plan
- [x] All 135 tests pass
- [x] Format check passes
- [x] Compiles against latest main

/claim #683

## Summary

Port Thrift support from ZIO Schema v1 to ZIO Schema 2 in a new top-level `schema-thrift` project.

### Implementation includes:
- `ThriftBinaryCodec` abstract class extending `BinaryCodec[A]`
- `ThriftFormat` object with `Deriver[ThriftBinaryCodec]`
- `ChunkTransport` for bridging Thrift transport with byte arrays/buffers
- Support for all 30 primitive types (Unit, Boolean, Byte, Short, Int, Long, Float, Double, Char, String, BigInt, BigDecimal, all java.time types, Currency, UUID)
- Record, variant, sequence, map, dynamic value, and wrapper support
- Recursive type support via lazy initialization
- Comprehensive test suite with **108 tests**

### Deriver Methods Implemented:
- `derivePrimitive` - All primitive types
- `deriveRecord` - Case classes encoded as Thrift structs
- `deriveVariant` - Sealed traits encoded as Thrift unions
- `deriveSequence` - Collections encoded as Thrift lists
- `deriveMap` - Maps encoded as Thrift maps
- `deriveDynamic` - DynamicValue support
- `deriveWrapper` - Wrapper types with validation

### Dependencies:
- `org.apache.thrift:libthrift:0.20.0`
- `jakarta.annotation:jakarta.annotation-api:3.0.0`

author: Pranjal6955
body:
## Summary
This PR introduces the `schema-thrift` module, porting **Thrift** serialization support to **ZIO Schema 2**. It provides a fully functional `ThriftDeriver` that automatically generates type-safe `ThriftBinaryCodec` instances for any ZIO Schema type, ensuring feature parity with the existing Avro implementation.

This is a direct port of the logic from the original [zio-schema-thrift](https://github.com/zio/zio-schema/tree/main/zio-schema-thrift/src) implementation, adapted for the new modular structure and ZIO Schema 2 APIs.

## Key Changes

### 1. New Module: `schema-thrift`
*   Established a new sbt project, `schema-thrift`, within the build configuration.
*   Integrating `org.apache.thrift:libthrift:0.20.0` and `jakarta.annotation:jakarta.annotation-api:3.0.0` as core dependencies.
*   Added a dependency on the `chunk` module to facilitate efficient, zero-copy byte handling.

### 2. Implementation: `ThriftDeriver` & `ThriftFormat`
*   **ZIO Schema 2 Migration:** Completely re-engineered the derivation logic to leverage the modern `Deriver` and `Reflect` APIs inherent to ZIO Schema 2.
*   **Robust Recursion Handling:** Implemented a thread-safe caching mechanism (using `ThreadLocal`) to handle recursive schemas for both **Records** and **Variants** (including `DynamicValue`), effectively preventing `StackOverflowError` during derivation.
*   **DynamicValue Support:** Enabled comprehensive support for `DynamicValue`, allowing for flexible, schema-less serialization and deserialization via Thrift.

### 3. Optimized Transport Layer
*   **`ThriftTransport`:** Developed a custom `TTransport` implementation backed by `zio.Chunk`. This allows for efficient serialization directly to and from ZIO Chunks, minimizing unnecessary array inclusions and buffer copying.

### 4. Comprehensive Type Support
The codec enables Thrift serialization for the full spectrum of ZIO Schema primitives:
*   **Standard Primitives:** Mapped to their native Thrift counterparts (`Boolean` -> `BOOL`, `Int` -> `I32`, `Long` -> `I64`, etc.) or appropriate fallbacks (`Char` -> `STRING`).
*   **Extended Numerics:** `BigInt` (serialized as `STRING`) and `BigDecimal` (serialized as a structured `STRUCT`).
*   **Miscellaneous:** `UUID` and `Currency` (serialized as `STRING`).
*   **Java Time:** Complete support for the `java.time` suite (e.g., `Instant`, `LocalDate`, `ZonedDateTime`), using standardized String or Struct representations.

### 5. Complex Structure Handling
*   **Collections:** Native support for `List`, `Vector`, `Set` (via `TType.LIST`/`TType.SET`), and `Map` (via `TType.MAP`).
*   **Records:** Case classes and nested records are seamlessly mapped to `TType.STRUCT`.
*   **Variants:**
    *   **Option:** Encoded using a specialized structure (Field 1: None, Field 2: Some) for reliable presence detection.
    *   **Either:** Handled as a union-like structure.
    *   **Sealed Traits:** fully supported as Thrift structs with discriminator fields for Enums and ADTs.

## Testing & Verification
*   **Test Suite:** Introduced `ThriftCodecSpec`, mirroring the rigorous coverage of `AvroFormatSpec`.
*   **Coverage:** 43 comprehensive tests validate functionality across all supported primitives, deep recursive structures, and `DynamicValue`.
*   **Result:** All tests passed successfully.

## Integration
*   The module is fully integrated into the root `build.sbt` structure.
*   Public API exposed via `zio.blocks.schema.thrift.ThriftFormat`.

## Example Usage

```scala
import zio.schema.Schema
import zio.blocks.schema.thrift.ThriftFormat
import zio.Chunk

case class Person(name: String, age: Int)
object Person {
  implicit val schema: Schema[Person] = Schema.gen
}

val person = Person("Alice", 30)
// Encodes directly to a ZIO Chunk[Byte]
val binary: Chunk[Byte] = ThriftFormat.encode(person) 

// Decodes back to a Person case class
val result: Either[String, Person] = ThriftFormat.decode[Person](binary)
```

/claim #681 
Fixes: #681
title: feat: Port Thrift Codec to ZIO Schema 2

## Summary

Implements a pure, algebraic migration system for ZIO Schema 2 that represents structural transformations between schema versions as first-class, serializable data.

Key features:
- `DynamicMigration` - fully serializable migration actions
- `Migration[A, B]` - type-safe wrapper with schema validation
- `MigrationBuilder` - fluent API for constructing migrations
- `Selector` macro - type-safe path expressions (`_.field`, `_.items.each`, `_.keys`, `_.values`)
- `MigrationExpr` - pure expressions for value transformations

## Bounty Requirements Checklist

- [x] `DynamicMigration` fully serializable
- [x] `Migration[A, B]` wraps schemas and actions
- [x] All actions path-based via `DynamicOptic`
- [x] User API uses selector functions for path specification
- [x] Macro validation in `.build()` method
- [x] `.buildPartial()` supported
- [x] Structural reverse implemented
- [x] Identity & associativity laws hold
- [x] Enum rename/transform supported (RenameCase, TransformCase)
- [x] Errors include path information
- [x] Comprehensive tests (39 tests)
- [x] Scala 2.13 and Scala 3.5+ supported

### Migration Actions Implemented

Record operations:
- AddField, DropField, RenameField, TransformField
- MandateField, OptionalizeField, ChangeFieldType
- JoinFields, SplitField

Enum operations:
- RenameCase, TransformCase

Collection operations:
- TransformElements, TransformKeys, TransformValues

## Test Plan

- [x] JVM tests pass (39 tests)
- [x] Scala 2.13.18 tests pass
- [x] Scala 3.3.6 tests pass
- [x] Migration laws verified (identity, associativity)
- [x] Selector macro tests pass on both Scala versions

Closes #519

author: hartmade
body:
## Summary

Complete implementation of the Json data type as specified in #679.

### Core Features
- **Json ADT**: Object, Array, String, Number, Boolean, Null with full pattern matching support
- **Navigation**: `get(path)`, `apply(path/index/key)` with `JsonSelection` for fluent chaining
- **Modification**: `set`, `delete`, `insert`, `modify` with `OrFail` variants
- **Transformations**: `transformUp`, `transformDown`, `transformKeys`, `filter`, `filterNot`
- **Folding**: `foldUp`, `foldDown` with `OrFail` variants
- **Utilities**: `normalize`, `sortKeys`, `dropNulls`, `dropEmpty`, `toKV`, `fromKV`

### String Interpolators
- `p"path.to.field[0]"` - Creates `DynamicOptic` paths at compile time
- `j"""{"key": "value"}"""` - Creates `Json` literals with interpolation support
- Platform-specific macro implementations for Scala 2 (blackbox) and Scala 3 (inline/quoted)

### Encoding/Decoding
- Parse from `String`, `CharSequence`, `Array[Byte]`, `Chunk[Byte]`, `ByteBuffer`, `Reader`
- Encode to `String`, `Array[Byte]`, `Chunk[Byte]`, `ByteBuffer`, `Writer`
- `WriterConfig` for indentation and unicode escaping options

### Type Classes
- `JsonDecoder[A]` and `JsonEncoder[A]` with low-priority schema derivation
- `JsonSelection` with `Either[SchemaError, Vector[Json]]` for error accumulation

### Interop
- `toDynamicValue` / `fromDynamicValue` conversions
- `as[A]` / `from[A]` typed encoding/decoding

### Placeholder Types (Out of Scope)
- `JsonPatch` with `patch`, `patchUnsafe`, `diff` stub methods
- `JsonSchema` with `check`, `conforms` stub methods

## Test Plan
- [x] All JVM tests pass (1151 tests)
- [x] All JS tests pass (74 tests)
- [x] All Native tests pass (74 tests)
- [x] `sbt check` formatting passes
- [x] Interpolators tested for paths and JSON literals
- [x] Cross-platform compilation verified

Closes #679

## Summary

Implements schema conversion type classes for transforming between types.

**Into[A,B]**: One-way conversion from A to B
- Primitive widening conversions (Byte→Short→Int→Long, Float→Double)
- Primitive narrowing conversions with range validation
- Collection conversions (List, Vector, Set) with element conversion
- Collection type conversions (List↔Vector↔Set)
- Option conversions
- Schema-based fallback derivation via DynamicValue

**As[A,B]**: Bidirectional conversion with round-trip guarantees
- Extends Into[A,B] with reverse conversion
- String ↔ char collection conversions
- `reverse()` method to flip direction
- Laws: round-trip preservation, reverse.reverse identity

**Extension methods:**
- `value.into[B]` for one-way conversion

## Summary

This replaces TypeName with TypeId, providing a richer type representation model.

**Key Changes:**
- `TypeId[A]` trait with comprehensive TypeRepr ADT supporting all Scala type forms
- Macro derivation via `TypeId.derived[A]` for both Scala 2.13 and 3.x
- `typeId` accessor on Schema and Reflect for runtime type identification
- Backward compatibility via `toTypeName`/`fromTypeName` conversions

**TypeRepr ADT supports:**
- Nominal: Regular named types (classes, traits, objects)
- Applied: Type constructors with args (List[Int], Map[K,V])
- Alias: Transparent type aliases
- Opaque: Opaque types (zio-prelude newtypes)
- Structural: Refinement types with members
- Intersection: A & B types
- Union: A | B types
- Function: A => B types
- Tuple: (A, B, C) types
- Singleton: x.type singleton types
- TypeParam: Type parameter references

**Features:**
- Structural equality with normalization (aliases expand)
- Subtype checking with parent traversal
- TypeBounds for type parameter constraints
- Stable hash codes for use as Map/Set keys

author: hartmade
body:
## Summary

Complete implementation of the Json data type as specified in issue #679.

This PR implements a comprehensive JSON ADT with full support for:
- **Type representation**: Object, Array, String, Number, Boolean, Null
- **Parsing**: Full JSON parser with error reporting (line/column/offset)
- **Serialization**: Compact and pretty printing with configurable options
- **Navigation**: Path-based access via DynamicOptic
- **Modification**: Immutable set, delete, insert, modify operations
- **Transformations**: sortKeys, dropNulls, normalize, merge strategies
- **DynamicValue interop**: Bidirectional conversion with ZIO Schema types

### Key Implementation Details

**JsonError** - Uses `Option[Long]` for offset, `Option[Int]` for line/column per spec

**JsonSelection** - Uses `Either[SchemaError, Vector[Json]]` with methods:
- `one`, `first`, `toArray`, `toVector`
- `oneUnsafe`, `firstUnsafe`
- `size`, `isEmpty`, `nonEmpty`
- `map`, `flatMap`, `filter`, `collect`
- `++` for combining selections

**Transform/Filter Methods** - Include path parameter:
- `transformUp(f: (DynamicOptic, Json) => Json)`
- `transformDown(f: (DynamicOptic, Json) => Json)`
- `transformKeys(f: (DynamicOptic, String) => String)`
- `filter(p: (DynamicOptic, Json) => Boolean)`
- `filterNot(p: (DynamicOptic, Json) => Boolean)`

**Fold Methods** - Include path parameter + OrFail variants:
- `foldUp[B](z: B)(f: (DynamicOptic, Json, B) => B)`
- `foldDown[B](z: B)(f: (DynamicOptic, Json, B) => B)`
- `foldUpOrFail[B](z: B)(f: (DynamicOptic, Json, B) => Either[JsonError, B])`
- `foldDownOrFail[B](z: B)(f: (DynamicOptic, Json, B) => Either[JsonError, B])`

**Additional Methods**:
- `query(p: (DynamicOptic, Json) => Boolean): JsonSelection`
- `project(paths: DynamicOptic*): Json`
- `partition(p: (DynamicOptic, Json) => Boolean): (Json, Json)`
- `printTo(writer: Writer)` variants
- `encodeToChunk` / `encodeToChunk(config: WriterConfig)`
- Parse/decode variants for `Reader`, `Chunk[Byte]`
- `parseUnsafe`, `decodeUnsafe`

**Number Type** - Lazy val accessors (throws on conversion error):
- `lazy val toInt`, `toLong`, `toFloat`, `toDouble`, `toBigInt`, `toBigDecimal`

**fields/elements** - Return `Seq` (empty for non-matching types)

**Equality** - Proper `hashCode`/`equals`/`toString` overrides

## Test Plan

- [x] All 59 JsonSpec tests pass
- [x] Full JVM test suite passes (1136 tests)
- [x] JS platform tests pass (59 tests)
- [x] Native platform tests pass (59 tests)
- [x] `sbt fmt` - code formatted
- [x] `sbt check` - linting passes

Closes #679

## Summary

This implements the ability to convert nominal type schemas to structural equivalents.

**Features:**
- `ToStructural[A]` type class for converting nominal types to structural types
- `StructuralValue` sealed trait representing runtime structural values
- Normalized type names with alphabetically sorted fields
- Support for products, sums, sequences, maps, and primitives
- Cross-compatible with Scala 2.13 and Scala 3
- Scala 3: `StructuralSelectable` using `Selectable` trait
- Scala 2: `StructuralDynamic` using `Dynamic` trait

**Limitations:**
- Recursive types throw `RecursiveTypeError` (by design)
- Sum types in Scala 2 throw `SumTypeScala2Error` (union types not available)

body:
## Summary

Adds TOON (Token-Oriented Object Notation) codec support to ZIO Blocks as a new `schema-toon` module, implementing the TOON Specification v3.0.

/claim #654

## Features

- Complete TOON v3.0 specification compliance
- All three array formats (inline, tabular, list) with auto-detection
- Configurable field/case name mapping
- ADT support with multiple discriminator strategies
- Full primitive type coverage (30+ types including java.time)
- Cross-platform: JVM, Scala.js, Scala Native

## Security

- Configurable max depth limit (default: 128)
- Configurable max document size (default: 64MB)

## Module Structure

- `ToonFormat` - BinaryFormat definition
- `ToonBinaryCodec` - Codec with encode/decode methods
- `ToonBinaryCodecDeriver` - Schema-based derivation
- `ToonReader` / `ToonWriter` - Parser and serializer
- `ReaderConfig` / `WriterConfig` - Configuration classes

## Test Plan

- [x] Basic primitive round-trip tests
- [x] Cross-platform compilation (JVM, JS, Native)
- [x] Integration with existing schema types

## Changes since PR #666

- Fixed scalafmt compliance (replaced Unicode arrows with ASCII)

Closes #654

author: zio-scala-steward[bot]
body:
## About this PR
📦 Updates [org.scoverage:sbt-scoverage](https://github.com/scoverage/sbt-scoverage) from `2.4.3` to `2.4.4`

author: Nanashi-lab
body:
/claim #516
/closes #516

### Uncompleted Review 

> Do you need Patch here, which can hold a DynamicPatch to be applied at that PatchPath (/DynamicOptic)? Need to think about if that can allow a more minimal patch in some cases or not.

I can add patch (nested) to `Operations`, so following

```scala
 DynamicPatch(Vector(
    DynamicPatchOp(Vector(Field("address"), Field("street")), Set("456 Elm")),
    DynamicPatchOp(Vector(Field("address"), Field("city")), Set("LA")),
    DynamicPatchOp(Vector(Field("address"), Field("zip")), Set("90002"))
  ))
```
will now become 
```scala
DynamicPatch(Vector(
    DynamicPatchOp(
      Vector(Field("address")),  // Path to parent
      Operation.Patch(           // Nested patch for children
        DynamicPatch(Vector(
          DynamicPatchOp(Vector(Field("street")), Set("456 Elm")),
          DynamicPatchOp(Vector(Field("city")), Set("LA")),
          DynamicPatchOp(Vector(Field("zip")), Set("90002"))
        ))
      )
    )
  ))
  ```
  This does not save a lot of serialization space,  Only saves space when `(N-1) * (D-1) > 1` where `N field changes` at `Depth D`, and is a lot of code over head. 
  
 ### Completed Review 
 
 Rest of the reviews have been addressed. 

-  Increment method is overload for all types 
-  addDuration methods are also overload, by using a dummy ladder (For all of below a dummy ladder is necessary because of type erasure, all of these become `Optic[Object]`)
-  addPeriod methods are also overloaded, by using a dummy ladder
-  Added Support for SeqOp, List, IndexedSeq, Vector and LazyList, again by using a dummy ladder
-  Removed implicit, and use the Schema from `Optic`. There are new implicit because of dummy ladders
- `AtMapKey` has been fixed, PatchPath has been completely removed from everywhere
- `StringOp` now has `Append` and `Modify`
- Made sure to add some comments for Scaladoc
- `Patch[S](dynamicPatch: DynamicPatch, schema: Schema[S])` is now private
- `Object Differ` has also been made private, and there is API for it in `DynamicValue`
- Fixed the MapOp, it now used `DynamicPatch`, that also fixes, the 1 at a time diff
- Moved all the PrimiativeOp into `Patch Object`, so they don't pollute the import

### Known Limitation
Schema instances for patch types are in Test. These schemas are in test code due to Scala 2 macro limitations.In Scala 2, Schema.derived macro calls cannot be used in the same compilation run as the type definitions. These are needed for json serialization.

Link to file - [schema/shared/src/test/scala/zio/blocks/schema/patch/PatchSchemas.scala](https://github.com/zio/zio-blocks/pull/671/files#diff-15d3892aa3b0a7f1cf0478c1d393aed34191afccdae141d777a297ee4474696c)

Possible solutions

- Change build.sbt, so that schema derive is compiled after the main compile (Preferred since this will come up again in Migration Ticket as well)
- Make a copy in Scala 3, and for scala 2 user would have to manually add the very long schema derive sequence
- or Manually write schema

```scala
object PatchSchemas {
  implicit val stringOpSchema: Schema[StringOp] = Schema.derived
  implicit val primitiveOpSchema: Schema[PrimitiveOp] = Schema.derived
  ...
  ...
  ```

author: hartmade
body:
## Summary
  Adds TOON (Token-Oriented Object Notation) codec support to ZIO Blocks as a new `schema-toon` module, implementing the TOON Specification v3.0.

  /claim #654

  ## Features
  - Complete TOON v3.0 specification compliance
  - All three array formats (inline, tabular, list) with auto-detection
  - Configurable field/case name mapping
  - ADT support with multiple discriminator strategies
  - Full primitive type coverage (30+ types including java.time)
  - Cross-platform: JVM, Scala.js, Scala Native

  ## Security
  - Configurable max depth limit (default: 128)
  - Configurable max document size (default: 64MB)

  ## Test Plan
  - [ ] Basic primitive round-trip tests
  - [ ] Cross-platform compilation (JVM, JS, Native)
  - [ ] Integration with existing schema types

author: elskow
body:
/claim #654

well this implementation are based on [Toon Format Spec](https://github.com/toon-format/spec/blob/main/SPEC.md). And the implementation are mirrored how the zio schema json done, but with some improvisation on the architecture. 

so the json looks like this
```
schema/shared/src/main/scala/zio/blocks/schema/json/
├── JsonBinaryCodec.scala          
├── JsonBinaryCodecDeriver.scala   (everything in one file)
├── DiscriminatorKind.scala
├── NameMapper.scala
├── ReaderConfig.scala
└── WriterConfig.scala
```

i went with a more modular approach for toon like this
```
schema-toon/src/main/scala/zio/blocks/schema/toon/
├── ToonBinaryCodec.scala
├── ToonBinaryCodecDeriver.scala   (just the deriver logic)
├── ToonFieldInfo.scala            (field handling)
├── ToonReader.scala
├── ToonWriter.scala
├── ToonCodecUtils.scala
├── codec/
│   ├── CodecDeriver.scala         (trait for cross-compilation)
│   ├── RecordCodecBuilder.scala   (record types)
│   ├── VariantCodecBuilder.scala  (sealed traits/enums)
│   ├── SequenceCodecBuilder.scala (arrays/lists)
│   └── MapCodecBuilder.scala      (maps)
└── ...configs etc
```

patterns & logic almost look the same as json, just organized differently so it's easier to find stuff. If yall had any issue with this kind of abstraction/builder pattern, lmk bout it
title: Add Toon Format Support for Zio Schema 2

author: wilmoore
body:
This PR adds an initial, JVM-only implementation of the TOON format as a separate
schema-toon module, following the schema-avro pattern.

Scope:
- New optional schema-toon module (no impact on existing users)
- Schema-based derivation via ToonFormat
- ToonBinaryCodec with writer + reader
- Support for primitives, records, inline arrays, and ADTs (Key discriminator)
- 22 tests covering round-trip correctness

Notes:
- This is a minimal, merge-safe MVP
- No JS/Native support yet
- Focused on round-trip correctness over full spec coverage
- Additional array formats and discriminator variants can follow incrementally

All tests pass locally.
title: Add initial TOON format support (schema-toon)

author: natinew77-creator
body: /claim #654 ## Summary This pull request implements complete TOON format support for ZIO Schema 2, fully compliant with **TOON Specification v3.0**. It includes a full codec deriver, streaming I/O components, and a comprehensive test suite covering **180 test cases**. ## Compliance & Verification - **Spec Version**: v3.0 (Official) - **Tests**: 180/180 Passing - **Key Features Verified**: - **Tabular Arrays**: Optimized `[N]{fields}:` format for uniform records. - **Inline Arrays**: Compact `[N]: 1,2,3` format for primitives. - **String Quoting**: "Minimal Quoting" rules (unquoted strings with spaces allowed, numeric strings quoted). - **Option Handling**: Transparent `null` / value encoding. - **Precision**: `BigDecimal`-backed double encoding (no trailing zeros). - **Unicode**: Full UTF-8 support including emoji. ## Implementation Overview ### Array Formats | Header | Format | Condition | |--------|--------|-----------| | `users[2]{id,name}` | Tabular | List of simple records | | `ids[3]` | Inline | List of primitives | | `items[0]` | Empty | Empty collection | | `data[2]` | List | Mixed/Complex types | ### Discriminator Support All three discriminator kinds are implemented: | Kind | Description | Output Example | |------|-------------|----------------| | Key | Wrapper object style (default) | `Cat:\n name: Whiskers` | | Field | Embedded discriminator field | `type: Cat\nname: Whiskers` | | None | Sequential case matching | `name: Whiskers` | ## Test Coverage The test suite fully integrates the **Official TOON Specification Fixtures** and includes: ### Categories - **Primitive Array Exact Match** - **Tabular Array Format Exact Match** - **Reviewer Reproduction Cases** (Option, Arrays, Indentation) - **Official Spec Compliance** (Primitives, Objects, Arrays, Unicode) - **Edge Cases** (Empty arrays, single-hyphen strings, deep nesting) ### Verified Data Types - **Primitives**: `Int`, `Long`, `Float`, `Double`, `Boolean`, `String` - **Collections**: `List`, `Vector`, `Set`, `Map` - **Time**: `LocalDate`, `Instant` - **Records**: `Point`, `Person`, `Department`, `DataSet` - **ADTs**: `Status` (Enum), `Shape` (Sealed Trait) Demo Video https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f16daefc-c0db-4760-b2f6-6b92af906f79
title: Complete TOON format implementation for ZIO Schema 2

author: Ashutosh0x
body:
## Overview
This PR implements the Schema Migration System (#519) and the full Algebraic Patch & Diffing System (#516).

### Algebraic Patch & Diffing (#516)
- **Comprehensive Operation ADT**: Implemented `Set`, `PrimitiveDelta` (Numeric & Temporal), `SequenceEdit`, and `MapEdit` in `DynamicPatch`.
- **Structural Recursive Diff**: Added automatic patch generation with **LCS algorithm** for strings and sequences.
- **Support for laws**: Verified Monoid laws (Identity, Associativity) and roundtrip laws via 35+ property-based tests.
- **Temporal & Large Numeric Deltas**: Added support for `Instant`, `Duration`, `BigInt`, and `BigDecimal`.
- **Cross-Platform**: Full verification on JVM and JS.

### Schema Migration System (#519)
- **Migration ADT**: Structural transformations including adding, removing, and renaming fields/cases.
- **Typed DSL**: Macro-enabled MigrationBuilder for Scala 2.13 and Scala 3.
- **Automatic Derivation**: Migration.derived[A, B] for automated structural mapping.
- **Algebraic Law Verification**: Formal tests for Identity, Associativity, and Structural Reverse.

Cc @jdegoes 

/claim #516
/claim #519
title: feat(schema): Implement Schema Migration System (#519)

author: AJ0070
body:
prev: #648 

## New Changes according to [review](https://github.com/zio/zio-blocks/pull/648#issuecomment-3733325293):

#### 1. Stringly-typed API & Source Compatibility

> *Stringly-typed API in Scala 2 which is NOT source-compatible with typed API in Scala 3*

**Fixed.** I removed all “stringly-typed” convenience methods (e.g., `addField("name", ...)`) from the public API.

- **Unified API:** Both Scala 2 and Scala 3 now expose the exact same selector-based signature:  
  `renameField(from: A => Any, to: B => Any)`.
- **Consistency:** This guarantees full source compatibility and enforces type-safety across both platforms using version-specific macros.

#### 2. Compile-Time Validation & Runtime Safety

> *There is no compile-time validation… weak System.err support…*

**Fixed.**

- **Compile-Time Validation (Scala 2 & 3):**  I implemented comprehensive macros for BOTH platforms (`MigrationBuilderValidation` for Scala 3, `MigrationBuilderMacros` for Scala 2) that perform deep AST traversal of the builder chain. They statically verify that:
  - All fields in the **Source** schema are handled (dropped / renamed / used).
  - All fields in the **Target** schema are produced (added / renamed).

  **Result:** Incomplete migrations now fail to compile with clear error messages (e.g.,  
  `Migration incomplete: target fields [age] are not produced`).

- **Runtime Safety:** I completely removed `System.err.println`. The runtime validation (used by Scala 2 or as a backup) now throws distinct exceptions (`IllegalStateException`) if a migration is invalid, ensuring visible failures.

#### 3. Structural Types & Intermediate Versions

> *No support for structural types… This work should not include the Schema#structural macro*

**Addressed.**

- **Explicit Removal:** As requested, I removed `StructuralSchemaDerivation.scala` and all related macro code from this PR.
- **Standardization:** The migration system now targets standard `Schema[A]` resolution (e.g., case classes). This allowed me to implement strict compile-time validation, which is much more reliable on concrete types. Once the structural macro is implemented in a separate PR, this system is designed to support it via the standard `Schema` interface.

#### 4. Error Handling (`SchemaError`)

> *Probably should use / expand SchemaError instead of MigrationError*

**Fixed.** I deleted `MigrationError` entirely.

- All components (`MigrationAction`, `DynamicMigration`, `Migration`) now return `Either[SchemaError, A]`.
- I integrated migration-specific failures (like **Type Mismatch** or **Evaluation Failed**) into `SchemaError`.

/claim #519

author: AJ0070
body:
### Overview

This PR implements a **pure, algebraic Migration System for ZIO Schema 2**. It represents structural transformations between schema versions as **first-class, serializable data**, enabling schema evolution and data versioning **without requiring runtime representations of past data versions**.

This implementation includes the **Core Architecture**, the **Typed User API**, and the platform-specific **Macros (Scala 2 & Scala 3)** required for a type-safe selector DSL.

---

### Features

#### Core Architecture

- **Pure Data**  
  Migrations are represented by the `DynamicMigration` ADT and `MigrationAction`s, which are fully serializable and introspectable.

- **Type Safety**  
  The user-facing `Migration[A, B]` API enforces source and target schemas.

- **Reversibility**  
  All actions support structural inversion (e.g., `dropField` is reversible because it stores a default value for restoration).

- **Error Types**  
  A comprehensive `MigrationError` hierarchy (`TypeMismatch`, `MissingField`, etc.) captures the exact `DynamicOptic` path where a failure occurred.

#### Selector-Based DSL

- Users can define migrations using type-safe selector functions  
  (e.g. `_.renameField(_.name, _.fullName)`).

##### Macro Implementation

- **Scala 3**
  - Uses quoted macros (`inline`) to extract paths.
  - Utilizes `StructuralSchemaDerivation` to define structural types on the fly.

- **Scala 2**
  - Uses `scala-reflect` (whitebox macros) to parse selector trees (`field`, `.when`, `.each`) into `DynamicOptic` nodes at compile time.

#### Verification Laws

- **Identity**  
  `Migration.identity[A]` is a no-op.

- **Associativity**  
  Composition of migrations is associative.

- **Structural Reverse**  
  Reversing a migration and reversing it again yields the original structure.

- **Semantic Inverse**  
  Validated via the *Best-Effort Semantic Inverse* property.

---

### Changes

#### Core  
`shared/src/main/scala/zio/blocks/schema/migration/`

- `Migration.scala`  
  Typed API generic over source and target.

- `DynamicMigration.scala`  
  Untyped, serializable core.

- `MigrationAction.scala`  
  Atomic operations (`AddField`, `DropField`, `Rename`, `TransformValue`, `Join`, `Split`, etc.).

- `MigrationBuilder.scala`  
  DSL builder implementation.

- `MigrationError.scala`  
  Error ADT with path support.

#### Scala 2 Integration  
`shared/src/main/scala-2/zio/blocks/schema/migration/`

- `MigrationBuilderMacros.scala`  
  Implements macro logic to split selector paths into `DynamicOptic` nodes.  
  Supports fields, `.when[Case]`, and `.each`.

- `MigrationBuilderPlatform.scala`  
  Wiring for the builder to use macros.

#### Scala 3 Integration  
`shared/src/main/scala-3/...`

- `StructuralSchemaDerivation.scala`  
  Implements `Schema.structural[T]` for refinement types and union types (tagged enums).

- **Validation**  
  Compile-time validation logic.

#### Integration

- `SchemaExpr.scala`  
  Added `DynamicLiteral` and `DefaultValue` for migration action support.

#### Testing  
`shared/src/test/scala/zio/blocks/schema/migration/`

- `MigrationBuilderSpec`  
  Verifies selector syntax execution for Scala 2 & Scala 3.

- `MigrationSpec`  
  Law checks and property-based tests.

---

### Verification

- **Test Suite**  
  Passed **664 tests**  
  (`sbt ++2.13.12 schemaJVM/test`), covering all implemented actions, laws, and selector syntax.

- **Style**  
  Code matches project style (`sbt scalafmtAll`).

---

### Notes

#### Structural Types

- **Scala 3**  
  Fully supported via `Schema.structural[T]`.

- **Scala 2**  
  Users should use case classes for version snapshots, as structural type derivation is a Scala 3–only feature in this PR.

#### Collection Macros

- Scala 2 implementation focuses on record operations (rename, drop, add) via selectors.
- Complex nested collection transforms (`transformValues`) are supported via `DynamicOptic` paths or Scala 3 selectors.

---

### AI Disclosure

- Used **Claude Sonnet** to understand the codebase, write comments, and ensure full test coverage.
- Used **ChatGPT** to rephrase the PR description.

/claim #519

author: natinew77-creator
title: Implement Schema Migration System for ZIO Schema 2

author: natinew77-creator
body:
## Overview

This PR implements a complete schema migration system for ZIO Schema 2, providing a pure, algebraic approach to transforming data between schema versions. The system centers around serializable migration actions that can be composed, reversed, and introspected.

## What's New

### Core Components

**MigrationAction** - A sealed trait defining 10 atomic transformations:
- Field operations: `DropField`, `RenameField`, `AddField`, `Optionalize`, `Mandate`
- Case operations: `RenameCase`, `RemoveCase`  
- Advanced transformations: `Join`, `Split`, `ChangeType`, `TransformCase`

**DynamicMigration** - A pure ADT representing migration pipelines:
- Composable via `++` operator (monoid instance)
- Reversible where semantically meaningful
- Fully serializable for offline processing
- Human-readable `describe` method

**Migration[A, B]** - Type-safe wrapper enforcing schema constraints:
- Automatic conversion between typed values and `DynamicValue`
- Compile-time safety through schema parameters
- Integration with ZIO Schema ecosystem

**MigrationBuilder** - Fluent DSL for constructing migrations:
- Scala 3: Type-safe field selectors using macros (`_.fieldName`)
- Scala 2: String-based API for compatibility
- Full IDE support with autocomplete

### Example Usage

```scala
// Define schema versions
case class PersonV0(firstName: String, lastName: String, age: Int)
case class PersonV1(fullName: String, age: Int, country: String)

implicit val v0Schema = Schema.derived[PersonV0]
implicit val v1Schema = Schema.derived[PersonV1]

// Build migration with type-safe API (Scala 3)
val migration = Migration.builder[PersonV0, PersonV1]
  .addField("fullName", "") // Will be populated by transform
  .dropField("firstName")   // String-based in Scala 2
  .dropField("lastName") 
  .addField("country", "USA")
  .build

// Apply migration
val oldPerson = PersonV0("John", "Doe", 30)
val result: Either[MigrationError, PersonV1] = migration(oldPerson)
```

## Testing

Comprehensive test suite with 28 passing tests:
- All 10 migration actions tested with success and failure cases
- Laws verified: Identity, Associativity, Reversibility
- Integration tests with typed `Migration[A, B]` API
- Edge cases and error handling covered

```
✅ 28/28 tests passing
✅ Zero compilation warnings
✅ Scala 2.13 & 3.x cross-compilation
```

## Design Decisions

### Pure Functional Core
All migrations are represented as pure, immutable data structures. This enables:
- Serialization for storage and transmission
- Introspection and analysis of migration structure
- Composition via simple data structure operations
- Testing without side effects

### Platform-Specific Traits
Used version-specific `MigrationBuilderPlatform` traits to provide the best experience on each Scala version while maintaining a unified public API.

### SchemaExpr Integration
Advanced actions like `Join`, `Split`, `ChangeType`, and `TransformCase` leverage the existing `SchemaExpr` system rather than duplicating expression logic.

## Files Added

**Implementation** (8 files):
- `migration/MigrationError.scala` - Error types with path tracking
- `migration/MigrationAction.scala` - 10 atomic migration operations
- `migration/DynamicMigration.scala` - Pure migration pipeline
- `migration/Migration.scala` - Type-safe wrapper
- `migration/MigrationBuilder.scala` - Builder DSL (shared)
- `migration/MigrationBuilderPlatform.scala` (Scala 3) - Macro-based selectors
- `migration/MigrationBuilderPlatform.scala` (Scala 2) - String-based API
- `migration/MigrationBuilderMacros.scala` (Scala 3) - Field extraction macros

**Tests** (2 files):
- `migration/MigrationSpec.scala` - Core action and law tests
- `migration/MigrationBuilderSpec.scala` - Builder DSL tests

## Note on Schema.structural

The `Schema.structural` macro mentioned in the issue is not included in this PR. Users can easily work around this limitation by using case classes for old schema versions:

```scala
// Instead of: type PersonV0 = { def name: String; def age: Int }
// Use:
case class PersonV0(name: String, age: Int)
```

This gives the same functionality - old versions don't need to be kept in the runtime since they're only referenced in migration definitions. I'm happy to add `Schema.structural` in a follow-up if desired.

## Verification

- ✅ All requirements from #519 implemented
- ✅ Zero warnings in Scala 2.13 and 3.x
- ✅ Comprehensive ScalaDoc on all public APIs
- ✅ Laws verified with property-based style tests
- ✅ Production-ready code quality

Closes #519
title: Implement Schema Migration System for ZIO Schema 2

author: Nanashi-lab
body:
/claim #516
/closes #516 

### Serializing DynamicOptic 

 `DynamicOptic.Node.AtMapKey[K]` has a generic type parameter K that makes it non-serializable without type information (requires TypeRegistry). We create `PatchPath` is a serializable replacement for `DynamicOptic.Node` that stores paths as pure data (DynamicValue instead of generics).

 1. Core Structure:
 ```scala
  final case class DynamicPatchOp(
    path: Vector[PatchPath],  // Where to apply
    operation: Operation       // What to apply
  )
```
  - Field("age") - navigate into record field
  - AtIndex(5) - navigate into sequence element
  - AtMapKey(key) - navigate into map value (key is DynamicValue, fully serializable)
  - Elements - traverse ALL sequence elements
  - Case("Active") - navigate into variant case
  - Wrapped - Pass through wrappers (transparent)

### Video 

Link to repl video - [Video](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1tCjmGZzcANBVle2a-n_PsrqsLtLQFxgW/view?usp=drive_link)

For video I am going with Repl example, below is the text of the Repl example.

```scala
scala> import zio.blocks.schema._
     | import zio.blocks.schema.patch._
     | import zio.blocks.schema.json._

scala> case class Address(city: String, zip: Int)
     | case class Person(name: String, age: Int, address: Address, skills: Vector[String])
// defined case class Address
// defined case class Person

scala> given addressSchema: Schema[Address] = Schema.derived
     | given personSchema: Schema[Person] = Schema.derived
// lazy val addressSchema: zio.blocks.schema.Schema[Address]
// lazy val personSchema: zio.blocks.schema.Schema[Person]

scala> object PersonOptics extends CompanionOptics[Person] {
     |   val age = optic(_.age)
     |   val address = optic(_.address)
     |   val skills = optic(_.skills)
     | }
// defined object PersonOptics

scala> object AddressOptics extends CompanionOptics[Address] {
     |   val city = optic(_.city)
     | }
     |
// defined object AddressOptics

scala> val alice = Person("Alice", 30, Address("NYC", 10001), Vector("Scala", "Java"))
// val alice: Person = Person(Alice,30,Address(NYC,10001),Vector(Scala, Java))

scala> val patch1 = Patch.set(PersonOptics.address(AddressOptics.city), "SF")

scala> val patch2 = Patch.append(PersonOptics.skills, Vector("Python", "Rust"))

scala> val combined = patch1 ++ patch2

scala> combined(alice, PatchMode.Strict)
// val res0: Either[zio.blocks.schema.SchemaError, Person] = Right(Person(Alice,30,Address(SF,10001),Vector(Scala, Java, Python, Rust)))

scala> val updated = Person("Alice", 31, Address("SF", 94102), Vector("Scala", "Java", "Rust"))
// val updated: Person = Person(Alice,31,Address(SF,94102),Vector(Scala, Java, Rust))

scala> val diffPatch = personSchema.diff(alice, updated)

scala> diffPatch(alice, PatchMode.Strict)
// val res1: Either[zio.blocks.schema.SchemaError, Person] = //Right(Person(Alice,31,Address(SF,94102),Vector(Scala, Java, Rust)))

scala> val empty = Patch.empty[Person]

scala> (empty ++ patch1)(alice, PatchMode.Strict) == patch1(alice, PatchMode.Strict)
// val res2: Boolean = true

scala> (patch1 ++ empty)(alice, PatchMode.Strict) == patch1(alice, PatchMode.Strict)
// val res3: Boolean = true

scala> val p1 = Patch.increment(PersonOptics.age, 1)

scala> val p2 = Patch.set(PersonOptics.address(AddressOptics.city), "LA")

scala> val p3 = Patch.append(PersonOptics.skills, Vector("Go"))

scala> ((p1 ++ p2) ++ p3)(alice, PatchMode.Strict) == (p1 ++ (p2 ++ p3))(alice, PatchMode.Strict)
//val res4: Boolean = true

scala> val invalidPatch = Patch.deleteAt(PersonOptics.skills, 10, 1)

scala> invalidPatch(alice, PatchMode.Strict)
// val res5: Either[zio.blocks.schema.SchemaError, Person] = Left(zio.blocks.schema.SchemaError: Delete range [10, 11) out of bounds for sequence of length 2 at: .skills)

scala> invalidPatch(alice, PatchMode.Lenient)
val res6: Either[zio.blocks.schema.SchemaError, Person] = //Right(Person(Alice,30,Address(NYC,10001),Vector(Scala, Java)))

scala> val multiPatch = Patch.deleteAt(PersonOptics.skills, 10, 1) ++ Patch.increment(PersonOptics.age, 1)
     | multiPatch(alice, PatchMode.Strict)
// val multiPatch: zio.blocks.schema.Patch[Person] = 
// val res7: Either[zio.blocks.schema.SchemaError, Person] = Left(zio.blocks.schema.SchemaError: Delete range [10, 11) out of bounds for sequence of length 2 at: .skills)

scala> multiPatch(alice, PatchMode.Lenient)
// val res8: Either[zio.blocks.schema.SchemaError, Person] = Right(Person(Alice,31,Address(NYC,10001),Vector(Scala, Java)))
```

### Known Limitation

Schema instances for patch types are in Test. These schemas are in test code due to Scala 2 macro limitations.In Scala 2, Schema.derived macro calls cannot be used in the same compilation run as the type definitions. These are needed for json serialization, there should be a way to do this, not sure how. 

```scala
object PatchSchemas {
  implicit val stringOpSchema: Schema[StringOp] = Schema.derived
  implicit val primitiveOpSchema: Schema[PrimitiveOp] = Schema.derived
  ...
  ...
  ```

author: nicefacer
body:
/claim #584

Overview
This PR introduces a new, pure zio.blocks.chunk module. It replicates the high-performance Chunk data structure from ZIO Core but removes all dependencies on the ZIO effect system (ZIO, UIO, Task, etc.) and other runtime libraries (like Izumi).

Key Features
Pure Implementation: Chunk is now a completely pure data structure. All effectful methods (e.g., mapZIO, filterZIO) have been removed.

Single-File Source: The entire Chunk implementation (including ChunkBuilder, NonEmptyChunk, etc.) is consolidated into a single file: chunk/shared/src/main/scala/zio/blocks/chunk/Chunk.scala.

Zero Runtime Dependencies: The chunk module has no library dependencies in the Compile scope. It only depends on zio-test for testing.

Cross-Platform: Supports both JVM and Scala.js.

Scala Versions: Configured for Scala 2.13.18 and Scala 3.7.4 (satisfying the 3.5+ requirement).

Benchmarks: Includes a JVM-only JMH benchmark suite to ensure performance parity.

Build Targets
chunkJVM: JVM artifact.
chunkJS: Scala.js artifact.
chunkBenchmarks: JMH benchmarks (JVM only).
Verification
To verify the changes, run the following commands (requires SBT):

Format Code:
sbt fmt

Run JVM Tests:

sbt chunkJVM/test

Run JS Tests:

sbt chunkJS/test

Run Benchmarks:

sbt chunkBenchmarks/jmh:run

Tests run locally:

sbt scalafmtAll
sbt clean chunkJVM/test chunkJS/test chunkBenchmarks/jmh:compile
Migration Notes
The package has changed from zio to zio.blocks.chunk.
NonEmptyChunk now extends Iterable[A] directly.
All ZIO-related methods are gone; users should use standard functional transformations (map, flatMap, foldLeft) and wrap in effects at the usage site if necessary.
Here is the video: https://youtu.be/oq_KoPRMJRU

author: nicefacer
body:
/claim #584



## Overview
This PR introduces a new, pure `zio.blocks.chunk` module. It replicates the high-performance `Chunk` data structure from ZIO Core but removes all dependencies on the ZIO effect system (`ZIO`, `UIO`, `Task`, etc.) and other runtime libraries (like Izumi).

## Key Features
- **Pure Implementation**: `Chunk` is now a completely pure data structure. All effectful methods (e.g., `mapZIO`, `filterZIO`) have been removed.

- **Single-File Source**: The entire `Chunk` implementation (including `ChunkBuilder`, `NonEmptyChunk`, etc.) is consolidated into a single file: `chunk/shared/src/main/scala/zio/blocks/chunk/Chunk.scala`.

- **Zero Runtime Dependencies**: The `chunk` module has no library dependencies in the `Compile` scope. It only depends on `zio-test` for testing.

- **Cross-Platform**: Supports both JVM and Scala.js.

- **Scala Versions**: Configured for Scala 2.13.18 and Scala 3.7.4 (satisfying the 3.5+ requirement).

- **Benchmarks**: Includes a JVM-only JMH benchmark suite to ensure performance parity.

## Build Targets
- `chunkJVM`: JVM artifact.
- `chunkJS`: Scala.js artifact.
- `chunkBenchmarks`: JMH benchmarks (JVM only).

## Verification
To verify the changes, run the following commands (requires SBT):

1.  **Format Code**:
    
    sbt fmt
    ```

2.  **Run JVM Tests**:
    
    sbt chunkJVM/test
    ```

3.  **Run JS Tests**:
    
    sbt chunkJS/test
    ```

4.  **Run Benchmarks**:

    sbt chunkBenchmarks/jmh:run
    ```
Tests run locally:
  - `sbt scalafmtAll`
  - `sbt clean chunkJVM/test chunkJS/test chunkBenchmarks/jmh:compile`

## Migration Notes
- The package has changed from `zio` to `zio.blocks.chunk`.
- `NonEmptyChunk` now extends `Iterable[A]` directly.
- All ZIO-related methods are gone; users should use standard functional transformations (`map`, `flatMap`, `foldLeft`) and wrap in effects at the usage site if necessary.

Here is the video: https://youtu.be/oq_KoPRMJRU
title: Replicate ZIO Chunk (from zio/zio) into here #584

author: SolariSystems
body:
## Summary

Complete implementation of the algebraic Patch/Diff system for ZIO Schema 2, as specified in #516.

### Changes

- **DynamicPatch**: Untyped patch operations on DynamicValue with full optic paths
- **Patch[A]**: Typed wrapper with schema-aware application
- **DeleteField**: Operation for proper record field removal
- **Property-based tests**: Verify `diff(a, b).apply(a) == b` law
- **Monoid laws**: Identity and associativity for patch composition
- **Error handling**: `applyOrFail` returns `Either[SchemaError, A]` with path information

### Addresses feedback from #528

- Removed old Patch.Pair approach entirely
- Added comprehensive property-based tests
- Full optic paths instead of field names
- Clean namespace organization

### Testing

All 43 tests passing including property-based tests for:
- Primitive, record, variant, sequence, map values
- Monoid identity and associativity laws
- DeleteField operation correctness

Fixes #516

author: Pranjal6955
body:
# PR Description

## 📝 Summary

This PR introduces a robust `TypeId` module to replace the legacy string-based `TypeName` system. It refactors the core `zio-blocks-schema` module to use `TypeId` as the primary mechanism for type identification, enabling more precise tracking of type parameters, opaque types, and nested ownership chains.

This change is 100% functionally complete and verified with **625 passing tests** across JVM platforms.

## ✨ Key Changes

### 1. New `TypeId` Module
- **Core Types**: Introduced `TypeId`, `Owner`, `TypeParam`, `TypeRepr`, `Member`, and `TermPath`.
- **Structure**: `TypeId` now supports:
    - **Nominal Types**: Fully qualified names with complete ownership chains (packages, terms, types).
    - **Aliases**: Logic for preserving and resolving type aliases.
    - **Opaque Types**: Dedicated support for Scala 3 opaque types.
- **Cross-Version Macros**: Implemented `TypeId` derivation macros for both **Scala 2.13** (using `scala-reflect`) and **Scala 3.5+** (using `scala.quoted`).

### 2. Schema Module Refactoring
- **Reflect Updates**: Updated `Reflect.scala`, `Schema.scala`, and related classes to use `TypeId` as the source of truth instead of `TypeName`.
- **Backward Compatibility**: `TypeName` has been retained as a compatibility wrapper that delegates to `TypeId`, ensuring minimal disruption to existing APIs.
- **Macro Updates**: Updated `SchemaVersionSpecific` macros to generate precise `TypeId` structures instead of flat strings.

### 3. Improvements & Fixes
- **Opaque Types**: Fixed issues with opaque type identity (e.g., distinguishing `Value <: Int` from `Int`).
- **ZIO Prelude**: Added robust support for ZIO Prelude `Newtype` derivation.
- **JSON Serialization**: Default JSON codec derivation now leverages fully qualified names for ambiguous unions, improving safety.

## 🧪 Test Coverage

- **Pass Rate**: 100% (625/625 tests passed).
- **Verified Scenarios**:
    - Generic Records & Tuples
    - Recursive Data Structures
    - Standard & Opaque Union Types
    - Higher-Kinded Types
    - JSON Round-trip Serialization

Fixes https://github.com/zio/zio-blocks/issues/471
/claim https://github.com/zio/zio-blocks/issues/471
title: feat: Introduce `typeid` module for robust type identification and migrate `schema` to use `TypeId` instead of `TypeName`
